d3bceb3e0772c5ae.plain_english: A $73M Kansas community bank, Small Business Bank of Lenexa, failed and was closed by state regulators, with the FDIC arranging for Farmers State Bank of Oakley to assume all deposits. Depositors face no losses and access is uninterrupted. This is a routine failure-resolution notice requiring no action.
d3bceb3e0772c5ae.title: The Farmers State Bank of Oakley, Kansas Assumes All Deposits of Small Business Bank, Lenexa, Kansas

24afe141606a8526.plain_english: Federal banking agencies issued a joint statement describing how examiners identify and handle highly sensitive information during examinations. It's procedural guidance on data handling, not a new compliance obligation for banks, so impact is largely informational.
24afe141606a8526.title: Examinations: Joint Statement on Identifying and Handling Highly Sensitive Information During Examinations

f451bc286cd7b83d.plain_english: Interagency guidance addresses lending to individuals not legally authorized to work in the US, covering underwriting, credit risk, and borrower eligibility/verification considerations. Any consumer lender should review origination and eligibility criteria against it. This is general lending-compliance guidance, not a fintech-specific topic.
f451bc286cd7b83d.title: Bank Supervision: Interagency Guidance on Lending to Individuals Not Legally Authorized to Work in the United States

4a9beedc5d2b8aa5.plain_english: The FDIC announced the failure and deposit assumption of a tiny ($3.73M asset) Indiana thrift by a local bank. This is a routine, isolated bank-failure notice with no policy, rule, or systemic implications for community banks or fintechs.
4a9beedc5d2b8aa5.title: Kentland Bank Assumes All Deposits of Kentland Federal Savings and Loan Association

4e6a9a03b4deb7b3.plain_english: The FDIC published its routine monthly list of state nonmember banks that received CRA evaluation ratings in April 2026. This is a standard administrative disclosure with no new rules or requirements.
4e6a9a03b4deb7b3.title: FDIC Issues List of Banks Examined for CRA Compliance

c0fe8d91e631d113.plain_english: The agencies published their annual list of distressed or underserved nonmetro middle-income geographies eligible for CRA revitalization/stabilization credit. Routine informational update with no new obligations; banks can use it to plan qualifying CRA activities.
c0fe8d91e631d113.title: Agencies Release List of Distressed or Underserved Nonmetropolitan Middle-Income Geographies

49be0e5f46f8f93c.plain_english: The FDIC released its routine monthly enforcement roundup for May 2026, including a new consent order against Connect Community Bank, civil money penalties, terminated orders, and individual prohibition/restitution actions. No new rules or obligations; community banks can review it for supervisory expectations.
49be0e5f46f8f93c.title: FDIC Publishes Enforcement Orders for May 2026

b0d41f1f118d428a.plain_english: The FDIC issued an NPRM revising deposit insurance assessment thresholds, rate schedules, and adjustments that could change what community banks pay in assessments. A separate resolution submission NPRM targets larger covered institutions and is largely irrelevant to sub-$10B banks. Watch the assessments proposal for cost impacts and file comments during the open period.
b0d41f1f118d428a.title: FDIC Board of Directors Meeting

addbe31e3b64d013.plain_english: This is a routine administrative notice announcing an open FDIC Board of Directors meeting to be held via webcast. It contains no substantive rule, guidance, or requirement affecting community banks or fintechs.
addbe31e3b64d013.title: Sunshine Act Notice: FDIC Board of Directors Meeting

5d8399828f3ca070.plain_english: The FDIC issued a memorial press release announcing the death of former Chairman William Isaac. This is a ceremonial notice with no regulatory changes, requirements, or deadlines.
5d8399828f3ca070.title: FDIC Statement on the Passing of Chairman William Isaac

119e7229a449e25f.plain_english: The OCC released its routine monthly enforcement action list for July 2026 with no details on the entities or violations involved. Worth a quick scan for any community-bank or BSA/AML actions, but nothing actionable can be determined from the summary alone.
119e7229a449e25f.title: OCC Announces Enforcement Actions for July 2026

695d44517ce001ab.plain_english: This is a milestone/PR statement marking the Comptroller's first year in office, offering general remarks on his priorities. It contains no new rules, guidance, or requirements affecting community banks or fintechs.
695d44517ce001ab.title: Jonathan V. Gould Marks One Year as Comptroller of the Currency

7f846e6f32a71e4c.plain_english: The OCC appointed the FDIC as receiver for Kentland Federal Savings and Loan Association, a small thrift in Indiana. This is a routine institution-specific receivership notice with no rule change or broad obligation for other institutions.
7f846e6f32a71e4c.title: OCC Appoints Receiver for Kentland Federal Savings and Loan Association, Kentland, Indiana

37737b8e84027d77.plain_english: The OCC announced a personnel promotion, naming Jamie Wilds as Deputy Comptroller for Supervision System and Analytical Support. This is a routine administrative announcement with no regulatory or supervisory changes affecting banks or fintechs.
37737b8e84027d77.title: OCC Announces Deputy Comptroller for Supervision System and Analytical Support

58f98bd53abba88c.plain_english: The OCC published its routine monthly list of CRA performance evaluations for 20 banks. This is an administrative disclosure, not a new rule or requirement, and imposes no action on institutions.
58f98bd53abba88c.title: OCC Releases CRA Performance Evaluations for 20  National Banks and Federal Savings Associations

3f95f17c20cb7eeb.plain_english: The OCC reported aggregate bank trading revenue of $16.3 billion for Q1 2026, up from prior periods. This is a routine statistical release dominated by large trading banks and imposes no requirements. It is not relevant to community banks or fintechs.
3f95f17c20cb7eeb.title: OCC Reports First Quarter 2026 Bank Trading Revenue

e0ee9c16c9569cc7.plain_english: The OCC published its routine quarterly report on first-lien mortgage performance in the federal banking system for Q1 2026. This is informational data reporting, not a new rule or requirement, and imposes no compliance obligations.
e0ee9c16c9569cc7.title: OCC Reports Mortgage Performance for First Quarter of 2026

3c69ae5bba5437e2.plain_english: The OCC issued version 2.0 of its Comptroller's Handbook booklet on allowances for credit losses under CECL (ASC 326), updating how examiners evaluate reserve adequacy and methodology. Banks should align their ACL models, assumptions and documentation with the refreshed exam expectations. This is supervisory guidance shaping credit-risk exams, not a new rule.
3c69ae5bba5437e2.title: Allowances for Credit Losses: Revised Comptroller’s Handbook Booklet and Rescissions

1880a2f7713db0ab.plain_english: FinCEN updated its Section 314(b) Fact Sheet to clarify how institutions can voluntarily share information about suspected fraud under the USA PATRIOT Act safe harbor. This is optional guidance with no new mandatory requirements, affecting any institution with BSA/AML obligations that wants to strengthen fraud detection through information sharing.
1880a2f7713db0ab.title: Notification: FinCEN Guidance on Voluntary Information Sharing

4e36810ca0395f63.plain_english: The OCC published a new consolidated 'Lending and Loan Portfolio Risk Management' booklet for the Comptroller's Handbook, replacing prior credit risk guidance and rescinding older materials. It shapes how examiners evaluate underwriting, concentrations, and portfolio risk management. Banks with lending operations should align their credit practices with the updated examiner expectations.
4e36810ca0395f63.title: Credit Risk: “Lending and Loan Portfolio Risk Management” Booklet of the Comptroller’s Handbook and Rescissions

e14b873dee380a3f.plain_english: The OCC proposed BSA/AML and sanctions compliance rules for OCC-supervised permitted payment stablecoin issuers under the GENIUS Act. Banks or fintechs issuing or sponsoring stablecoins would face defined AML/sanctions program obligations. This is a comment-stage opportunity to shape the requirements before finalization.
e14b873dee380a3f.title: GENIUS Act: Anti-Money Laundering/Countering the Financing of Terrorism and Sanctions Compliance: Notice of Proposed Rulemaking

59b74b85ee313ea7.plain_english: The OCC clarified the procedural criteria it applies when deciding on filings such as charter and merger applications. This is relevant to fintechs pursuing bank charters, as it clarifies how the OCC evaluates these submissions, but it is procedural and imposes no new substantive requirements.
59b74b85ee313ea7.title: Filing Decision Process

643d249e069ccd66.plain_english: The OCC reissued its policy statement on minority depository institutions, revising the MDI definition to align with FIRREA and removing obsolete language. This is a definitional/administrative update with no new compliance obligations for community banks or fintechs unless they are MDIs seeking OCC support programs.
643d249e069ccd66.title: Minority Depository Institutions: Reissuance of Policy Statement

32ba9717ab6c6b72.plain_english: Regulators finalized common machine-readable data standards for regulatory reporting under the Financial Data Transparency Act. Community banks will eventually need to align their reporting systems to these standardized formats. It's a reporting-infrastructure change, not a substantive lending or AML mandate, with a long runway before implementation.
32ba9717ab6c6b72.title: Financial Data Transparency Act of 2022: Final Rule
32ba9717ab6c6b72.fr_title: Financial Data Transparency Act Joint Data Standards
32ba9717ab6c6b72.fr_dates_text: Effective date: The joint rule is effective on October 1, 2026. At the effective date, the joint rule will not change any reporting requirements without further action by the agencies.

e65dc45d2fd15c6f.plain_english: The OCC is proposing new weekly and quarterly reporting forms for permitted payment stablecoin issuers (including foreign issuers) registered under the GENIUS Act. Any bank or fintech pursuing OCC-jurisdiction stablecoin issuance should evaluate the new reporting burden and comment. Community banks not issuing stablecoins are largely unaffected.
e65dc45d2fd15c6f.effective_or_comment_date: Comments due within 60 days of Federal Register publication
e65dc45d2fd15c6f.title: GENIUS Act: Reporting Forms and Instructions for Permitted Payment Stablecoin Issuers Subject to the Jurisdiction of the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
e65dc45d2fd15c6f.fr_title: Agency Information Collection Activities: Proposed Information Collection; Reporting Forms and Instructions for Permitted Payment Stablecoin Issuers Subject to the Jurisdiction of the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency; Comment Request
e65dc45d2fd15c6f.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received by August 11, 2026.

b5c44e60f9484370.plain_english: The Fed took an individual enforcement action against a former chief lending officer of a state member bank, likely involving lending misconduct. The impact is limited to the named individual and carries no industry-wide obligations.
b5c44e60f9484370.title: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement action with former chief lending officer of Heritage State Bank

40f8d9098b8dcce8.plain_english: These are routine minutes of the Federal Reserve Board's discount rate meetings, an administrative disclosure of internal monetary policy deliberations. They impose no new requirements on community banks or fintechs.
40f8d9098b8dcce8.title: Minutes of the Board's discount rate meetings on June 8 and June 17, 2026

1a39c3d66d42f19e.plain_english: The Federal Reserve announced leadership and objectives for task forces focused on the conduct of monetary policy. This is an internal administrative and policy-framework matter, not a regulatory change affecting bank or fintech compliance obligations.
1a39c3d66d42f19e.title: Federal Reserve announces the leadership and objectives of its task forces to advance the conduct of monetary policy

d4d6c518afcd8f09.plain_english: The Fed issued an enforcement action against two bank holding companies, TS Banking Group and TS Contrarian Bancshares, but the summary gives no detail on the underlying violations. Without specifics on whether it involves BSA/AML, capital, or governance, there is no actionable takeaway beyond noting the action exists.
d4d6c518afcd8f09.title: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement action with TS Banking Group, Inc. and TS Contrarian Bancshares, Inc.

b748bca70737e3bf.plain_english: These are the FOMC minutes summarizing monetary policy discussions from the June 2026 meeting. They contain no regulatory requirements, rules, or compliance obligations affecting community banks or fintechs. While rate decisions have general economic impact, this is not a supervisory or compliance matter within our focus.
b748bca70737e3bf.title: Minutes of the Federal Open Market Committee, June 16-17, 2026

437d451dd51bd6d0.plain_english: The Fed proposes amending its AML program requirements for banks, likely to align with AML Act modernization. Banks would need to revisit BSA/AML program design and internal controls once finalized; comment now to influence the outcome. This is general BSA/AML rulemaking, not fintech-specific.
437d451dd51bd6d0.title: Federal Reserve Board requests comment on a proposal to amend its requirements for banks to maintain anti-money laundering programs

fac7edb635d62bc0.plain_english: The Fed issued a new enforcement action against Small Business Bank (details unspecified) and terminated prior actions against BNP Paribas entities and Community Bankshares, signaling those issues were resolved. No new obligations flow to other institutions; relevance is limited to the named parties.
fac7edb635d62bc0.title: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement action with Small Business Bank and announces termination enforcement actions with BNP Paribas S.A., BNP Paribas USA, Inc., BNP Paribas Securities Corp., and Community Bankshares, Inc.

a15037512ece61b0.plain_english: The Federal Reserve released initial findings from its 2025 triennial payments study, a periodic statistical report on payment trends. It carries no new rules, requirements, or compliance obligations for banks or fintechs. It may be informative background reading but does not require action.
a15037512ece61b0.title: Federal Reserve issues initial findings from its 2025 triennial payments study

fd8bbcbd2abe91e6.plain_english: The Fed created a supervisory nonobjection process requiring state member banks to notify and obtain no-objection before engaging in dollar token/stablecoin activities using distributed ledger technology. Banks pursuing tokenized dollar or stablecoin-related payment/BaaS products must get prior Fed sign-off and demonstrate adequate risk controls before proceeding.
fd8bbcbd2abe91e6.title: CA 23-5 / SR 23-8

7f9d6348461dcedd.plain_english: The Fed clarified that the 314(b) safe harbor covers voluntary information sharing about fraud, not just money laundering and terrorist financing. Banks now have clearer legal comfort to exchange fraud-related data through 314(b) channels, which may warrant updates to BSA/AML and fraud program policies to take advantage of the expanded scope.
7f9d6348461dcedd.title: SR 26-3

42239265d26aedf6.plain_english: The Fed revised its model risk management guidance, updating expectations for developing, validating, and governing models used in credit risk, AML, and pricing—including AI/ML and vendor models. Banks relying on models (impact scaling with complexity) should reassess validation and governance against the new standards. This is general supervisory guidance, not a fintech-specific rule, though sponsor banks using vendor/AI models are squarely affected.
42239265d26aedf6.title: SR 26-2

efa72c43614ad614.plain_english: This is an older Fed supervisory letter regarding inspections of merger and acquisition activities, focused on bank holding company M&A supervision. It does not touch on our focus areas of BaaS/sponsor-bank arrangements, prepaid/FBO accounts, consumer lending, BSA/AML, or internal audit obligations for community banks and fintechs.
efa72c43614ad614.title: SR 94-22 (FIS)

11f72428ff5b2033.plain_english: This is a decades-old Fed SR letter describing the general purpose and framework for formal enforcement actions. It introduces no new obligations and is background/administrative in nature.
11f72428ff5b2033.title: SR 90-36 (FIS)

c49e5d5b12bffae7.plain_english: The Fed clarified how investment funds and their portfolio companies are treated under Regulation O insider-lending rules and Part 363 audit/reporting requirements. Banks with fund-affiliated directors or investors should assess whether related-party lending or audit obligations are triggered, though most banks under $10B fall below Part 363 thresholds.
c49e5d5b12bffae7.title: SR 25-6

7405a779ee997514.plain_english: An administrative Fed CA letter clarifying how the Fed communicates with banks about its consumer compliance supervision program. It imposes no new substantive requirements and is relevant only as context for Fed-supervised banks on how exam communications work.
7405a779ee997514.title: CA 25-1

53b1350391aba9fa.plain_english: SR 25-5 clarifies how mutual-form banking organizations issue regulatory capital instruments. It only matters to the subset of community banks organized in mutual form that are raising or structuring capital; stock-form banks and fintechs are unaffected.
53b1350391aba9fa.title: SR 25-5

e419e9f5780fd8ec.plain_english: The Fed reissued FAQs clarifying suspicious activity report (SAR) filing and documentation expectations under BSA/AML rules. BSA/compliance officers should compare the guidance against current SAR procedures for interpretive shifts. This is general BSA guidance applicable to all banks, though sponsor-bank AML programs supporting fintech partners will also want to align.
e419e9f5780fd8ec.title: SR 25-4

5a2da86ab8d6d7b7.plain_english: The Fed distributed FinCEN's special measures against entities designated as primary money laundering concerns, requiring banks to screen for and block covered transactions or accounts. BSA/AML programs must be updated to implement these restrictions, with direct enforcement exposure for noncompliance.
5a2da86ab8d6d7b7.title: SR 25-3

001b3b9f385921a5.plain_english: Multiple agencies including the CFPB adopted uniform standards for reporting financial data, likely tied to the Financial Data Transparency Act. Institutions will need to align their reporting formats and processes with the new standardized requirements once implementation details take effect.
001b3b9f385921a5.title: CFPB Announces Joint Final Rule on Adopting Uniform Standards for Reporting Financial Data

68a2c63d3190b71a.plain_english: The CFPB says it will overhaul its consumer complaint portal to address flaws in effectiveness and data utility, potentially altering how institutions receive, respond to, and are judged on complaints. No new requirements or deadlines are announced, so this is early-stage signaling rather than an actionable change.
68a2c63d3190b71a.title: The CFPB is Correcting Flaws to Restore Integrity and Utility to the Consumer Complaint System

2779d5ad7604aaa8.plain_english: The CFPB pressed Bilt to compensate consumers harmed during its migration to a new bank partner, signaling heightened scrutiny of BaaS/sponsor-bank arrangements—especially the consumer risks that surface when a fintech switches sponsor banks. Sponsor banks and fintech partners should build consumer-protective transition plans, as botched migrations can now draw CFPB action and reputational/compliance fallout.
2779d5ad7604aaa8.title: The CFPB Works To Ensure Bilt Consumers Are Made Whole

9f8d38042d9a4534.plain_english: The CFPB published 2025 HMDA mortgage lending data on the FFIEC platform. This is a routine data-availability announcement with no new obligations for reporters.
9f8d38042d9a4534.title: 2025 HMDA Data on Mortgage Lending Now Available

4e59f07321efc0eb.plain_english: This appears to be a research or opinion piece estimating the CFPB's cost to consumers, not a rule, guidance, or enforcement action. It imposes no new requirements on community banks or fintechs and does not change compliance obligations.
4e59f07321efc0eb.title: Estimating the Cost of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau to Consumers

1318f7bfc0ad2270.plain_english: The CFPB and DOJ withdrew their joint statement clarifying how creditors may consider immigration/citizenship status under ECOA, removing prior interpretive cover without imposing any new requirement. Lenders that underwrite based on citizenship or immigration status now face more ambiguity on fair-lending exposure and should re-review those policies.
1318f7bfc0ad2270.title: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau and the Department of Justice Withdraw Joint Statement on Fair Lending and Credit Opportunities for Noncitizen Borrowers

cfe46ed81096505d.plain_english: The CFPB and FRB published the annual inflation-adjusted thresholds determining which consumer credit and lease transactions fall under Regulation Z (TILA) and Regulation M for 2026. This is a routine annual update with no new substantive obligations; consumer lenders should refresh their coverage cutoffs.
cfe46ed81096505d.effective_or_comment_date: Applicable for 2026 transactions (specific date not stated)
cfe46ed81096505d.title: Agencies Announce Dollar Thresholds for Applicability of Truth in Lending and Consumer Leasing Rules for Consumer Credit and Lease Transactions

5343774a08430fc6.plain_english: The annual inflation adjustment raises the HPML appraisal exemption threshold to $34,200 for 2026 (up from $33,500), so mortgage loans below that amount remain exempt from the special higher-priced-mortgage-loan appraisal rules. This is a routine yearly recalibration for mortgage lenders' compliance operations.
5343774a08430fc6.title: Agencies Announce Dollar Thresholds for Smaller Loan Exemption from Appraisal Requirements for Higher-priced Mortgage Loans

c7c347277784ded8.plain_english: The CFPB's Supervision Division announced a softer, less enforcement-driven tone for exams via a 'Humility Pledge.' There are no new rules, requirements, or dates—this is signaling only and may suggest a lighter-touch supervisory posture for CFPB-supervised institutions.
c7c347277784ded8.title: CFPB’s Supervision Division Releases New 'Humility Pledge'

e8341c5c6befefb9.plain_english: The CFPB told a court that DOJ's OLC concluded it cannot currently draw its Federal Reserve funding under Dodd-Frank, casting doubt on its operational capacity. No existing legal obligations change, but pending rulemakings, supervision, and enforcement cadence may slow or stall in the near term. Watch this for signals on timing of CFPB-driven rules that affect lenders and fintechs.
e8341c5c6befefb9.title: CFPB Notifies Court it Cannot Lawfully Draw Funds from the Federal Reserve

9f058e11cde78627.title: Financial Data Transparency Act Joint Data Standards
9f058e11cde78627.fr_title: Financial Data Transparency Act Joint Data Standards
9f058e11cde78627.fr_dates_text: Effective date: The joint rule is effective on October 1, 2026. At the effective date, the joint rule will not change any reporting requirements without further action by the agencies.

105717c20098fd7d.plain_english: The CFPB finalized amendments to its Section 1071 small business lending data collection rules under Reg B through a reconsideration rulemaking, potentially changing compliance thresholds, required data fields, and reporting timelines. This hits any lender making small business loans, including community banks and non-bank/fintech small business lenders. The changes may relax or delay existing 1071 obligations.
105717c20098fd7d.title: Small Business Lending under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act (Regulation B)
105717c20098fd7d.fr_title: Small Business Lending Under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act (Regulation B)
105717c20098fd7d.fr_dates_text: This final rule is effective on June 30, 2026. The compliance date for the rule is January 1, 2028.

7b62c534d6f832bf.plain_english: The CFPB finalized amendments to Regulation B (ECOA) affecting disparate-impact standards, applicant discouragement, and special purpose credit programs. This hits all consumer lenders, changing fair-lending liability exposure and how targeted credit programs can be designed. Fair-lending and compliance teams should reassess disparate-impact analysis and marketing practices.
7b62c534d6f832bf.title: Equal Credit Opportunity Act (Regulation B)
7b62c534d6f832bf.fr_dates_text: This final rule is effective July 21, 2026.

5a01398d60cd51e9.plain_english: The CFPB is rescinding a purely procedural rule that required state officials to notify the CFPB before enforcing the CFPA. This is an internal administrative/procedural change affecting government coordination, not bank or fintech compliance obligations.
5a01398d60cd51e9.title: Rescission of State Official Notification Rules

695faca6670287ba.plain_english: The CFPB withdrew a direct final rule that would have rescinded procedures for states to notify the Bureau when enforcing the Consumer Financial Protection Act. This is a procedural notification matter between state officials and the CFPB, with no direct compliance obligations for banks or fintechs.
695faca6670287ba.title: Rescission of State Official Notification Rules; Withdrawal

75a88dd058fca3e5.plain_english: The CFPB rescinded 2022 and 2023 amendments to its internal Rules of Practice for administrative adjudication proceedings. This is a procedural change affecting how the CFPB conducts its own enforcement adjudications, not a substantive compliance obligation for banks or fintechs.
75a88dd058fca3e5.title: Rules of Practice for Adjudication Proceedings

57a8e39c7cbcb263.plain_english: The CFPB finalized its annual CPI-indexed adjustment to the appraisal exemption threshold for higher-priced mortgage loans under TILA 129H. Lenders originating HPMLs should update their systems to the new threshold; this is a routine inflation adjustment, not a substantive policy change.
57a8e39c7cbcb263.title: Appraisals for Higher-Priced Mortgage Loans Exemption Threshold
57a8e39c7cbcb263.fr_title: Appraisals for Higher-Priced Mortgage Loans Exemption Threshold
57a8e39c7cbcb263.fr_dates_text: This final rule is effective January 1, 2026.

2760d43991b31c2d.plain_english: The CFPB's routine annual CPI adjustment raises the Reg Z exemption threshold for consumer credit from $71,900 to $73,400, meaning consumer credit above that amount is generally exempt from Reg Z. Consumer lenders should update systems and disclosures to reflect the new figure.
2760d43991b31c2d.title: Truth in Lending (Regulation Z)
2760d43991b31c2d.fr_dates_text: This final rule is effective January 1, 2026.

776136957fc50a18.plain_english: The CFPB set the 2026 maximum fee a consumer reporting agency may charge a consumer for a file disclosure under FCRA section 609. This is a routine annual inflation adjustment affecting credit reporting agencies, not community banks or fintechs as lenders.
776136957fc50a18.title: Fair Credit Reporting Act Disclosures

6e9553b3fd728740.plain_english: The CFPB's annual inflation adjustment raises the Regulation M consumer leasing exemption threshold to $73,400 (from $71,900); leases above that stay exempt from disclosure requirements. Any lender offering consumer leases just updates its threshold reference—no substantive change.
6e9553b3fd728740.title: Consumer Leasing (Regulation M)
6e9553b3fd728740.fr_dates_text: This final rule is effective January 1, 2026.

8cdced10451f21f5.plain_english: FinCEN proposes expanding its section 311 primary-money-laundering-concern definition of Huione Group to include H-Pay Service PLC and to add a 'successor entity' definition, while keeping the existing special measure at 31 CFR 1010.664. BSA/AML programs should update sanctions/transaction screening to capture the newly named entity so covered transactions are blocked; this hits money-transmitter and BaaS/payments-focused fintechs handling cross-border flows especially hard.
8cdced10451f21f5.title: Definition of Huione Group, a Financial Institution Operating Outside the United States of Primary Money Laundering Concern
8cdced10451f21f5.fr_title: Definition of Huione Group, a Financial Institution Operating Outside the United States of Primary Money Laundering Concern
8cdced10451f21f5.fr_dates_text: Written comments on the notice of proposed rulemaking must be submitted on or before July 27, 2026.

bd930c2d87ff0d8b.plain_english: FinCEN and the banking agencies propose classifying GENIUS Act-permitted payment stablecoin issuers as BSA financial institutions, subjecting them to customer identification program (CIP) requirements. This pulls stablecoin issuers fully into the BSA/AML regime and hits any bank or fintech issuing or sponsoring stablecoin activity, which would face formal AML/CIP obligations.
bd930c2d87ff0d8b.title: Agencies Request Comment on Customer Identification Program Requirements for Permitted Payment Stablecoin Issuers
bd930c2d87ff0d8b.fr_title: Permitted Payment Stablecoin Issuer Customer Identification Program
bd930c2d87ff0d8b.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received by August 21, 2026.

ec16e1b7d892c27d.plain_english: FinCEN is seeking comments on a routine Paperwork Reduction Act renewal, without change, of the MSB registration requirements and Form 107. Nothing substantive is changing—existing MSB registration, biennial renewal, and agent-list obligations remain the same. This is an administrative comment request with no new requirements for banks or fintechs.
ec16e1b7d892c27d.title: Agency Information Collection Activities; Proposed Renewal; Comment Request; Renewal Without Change of the Registration of Money Services Businesses Regulation and FinCEN Form 107

1972acaa6a3f04f2.plain_english: FinCEN amended its June 2025 special-measure order against Mexico's CIBanco (named a primary money laundering concern linked to opioid trafficking) to add a narrow exception permitting fund transmittals needed for the Mexican government to liquidate the bank. Institutions with BSA/AML payment-screening controls touching CIBanco-related flows must update their filters to reflect the newly permitted transactions. Impact is limited to those with actual CIBanco exposure.
1972acaa6a3f04f2.title: Imposition of Special Measure Prohibiting Certain Transmittals of Funds Involving CIBanco S.A., Institución de Banca Multiple; Amendment
1972acaa6a3f04f2.fr_title: Imposition of Special Measure Prohibiting Certain Transmittals of Funds Involving CIBanco S.A., Institución de Banca Multiple; Amendment
1972acaa6a3f04f2.fr_dates_text: FinCEN is amending the order published at 90 FR 27770 (June 30, 2025), as amended by 90 FR 30826 (July 11, 2025) and 90 FR 40974 (August 22, 2025), as of April 16, 2026.

f724571152080c4e.plain_english: FinCEN proposes a major overhaul of AML/CFT program rules under the AML Act, requiring 'effective, risk-based and reasonably designed' programs and expanding FinCEN's supervisory and enforcement role. This affects all BSA-covered institutions and could reshape risk assessments, program design, and exam expectations. Firms should prepare comments and anticipate significant compliance changes.
f724571152080c4e.title: Anti-Money Laundering and Countering the Financing of Terrorism Programs
f724571152080c4e.fr_title: Anti-Money Laundering and Countering the Financing of Terrorism Programs
f724571152080c4e.fr_dates_text: Comments must be submitted on or before September 8, 2026.

2cff54faa2eb531d.plain_english: FinCEN proposes AML/CFT and sanctions compliance program requirements specifically for permitted payment stablecoin issuers, implementing the stablecoin regulatory framework. Any fintech or bank subsidiary issuing payment stablecoins would need to build BSA/AML and sanctions programs meeting these standards. Firms in or entering the stablecoin space should review and consider commenting.
2cff54faa2eb531d.title: Permitted Payment Stablecoin Issuer Anti-Money Laundering/Countering the Financing of Terrorism Program and Sanctions Compliance Program Requirements
2cff54faa2eb531d.fr_title: Permitted Payment Stablecoin Issuer Anti-Money Laundering/Countering the Financing of Terrorism Program and Sanctions Compliance Program Requirements
2cff54faa2eb531d.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received by June 9, 2026.

8d1b25ccde27bcac.plain_english: FinCEN proposes a formal BSA/AML whistleblower program with financial incentives and anti-retaliation protections for those reporting BSA and sanctions violations. This raises whistleblower exposure for any institution with BSA/AML obligations, elevating the value of strong internal reporting, compliance, and audit controls. Still a proposal open for comment, not yet binding.
8d1b25ccde27bcac.title: Whistleblower Incentives and Protections
8d1b25ccde27bcac.fr_dates_text: Written comments on this proposed rule must be submitted on or before June 1, 2026.

9cb2687ec984c500.plain_english: FinCEN issued a Geographic Targeting Order lowering the CTR/recordkeeping and ID-verification threshold to $1,000 (from $10,000) for MSBs operating in specified southwest border areas. This hits MSBs directly and any fintech or sponsor-bank programs that partner with those MSBs, sharply increasing BSA/AML reporting and recordkeeping volume.
9cb2687ec984c500.title: Geographic Targeting Order Imposing Recordkeeping and Reporting Requirements on Certain Money Services Businesses Along the Southwest Border

b205a9f3837de589.plain_english: FinCEN proposes a Section 311 special measure prohibiting US financial institutions from maintaining correspondent accounts for Switzerland's MBaer Merchant Bank and requiring screening to block MBaer-linked transactions. This only affects institutions with foreign correspondent relationships, which is uncommon among small community banks.
b205a9f3837de589.title: Proposal of Special Measure Regarding MBaer Merchant Bank AG as a Financial Institution Operating Outside of the United States of Primary Money Laundering Concern
b205a9f3837de589.fr_title: Proposal of Special Measure Regarding MBaer Merchant Bank AG as a Financial Institution Operating Outside of the United States of Primary Money Laundering Concern
b205a9f3837de589.fr_dates_text: Written comments on the notice of proposed rulemaking must be submitted on or before April 1, 2026.

754a0e199d49581c.plain_english: FinCEN is soliciting nominations for membership in the Bank Secrecy Act Advisory Group for three-year terms. This is a routine administrative/membership notice with no new compliance obligations for community banks or fintechs.
754a0e199d49581c.title: Bank Secrecy Act Advisory Group; Solicitation of Application for Membership

ffb5aa020f180538.plain_english: NCUA is renewing existing information collections and submitting them to OMB under the Paperwork Reduction Act. This is a routine administrative PRA notice with no substantive change to requirements.
ffb5aa020f180538.title: Renewal of Agency Information Collections for Comments Request: Proposed Collections

d6840359e8948f3b.plain_english: NCUA opened a funding round under the Community Development Revolving Loan Fund, offering loans (and typically grants) to low-income-designated credit unions. Relevant only to qualifying CDCUs seeking this funding; no rule change.
d6840359e8948f3b.title: Community Development Revolving Loan Fund
d6840359e8948f3b.fr_dates_text: This NOFO is effective on the date of publication in the Federal Register. NCUA accepts applications on a rolling basis. Applicants will be notified of NCUA's decision after the agency's review is completed. The application review process will take approximately 180 calendar days.

205aea27fe58e56f.plain_english: The NCUA finalized a rule eliminating reputation risk from its supervisory framework for credit unions, implementing Executive Order 14331. Examiners can no longer cite reputation risk as a basis for supervisory action against NCUA-supervised credit unions.
205aea27fe58e56f.effective_or_comment_date: NCUA ceased examining for reputation risk effective September 25, 2025
205aea27fe58e56f.title: Prohibition on the Use of Reputation Risk
205aea27fe58e56f.fr_title: Prohibition on the Use of Reputation Risk
205aea27fe58e56f.fr_dates_text: This final rule is effective on July 27, 2026.

e2b31fadda63d0e9.plain_english: This is a routine NCUA Sunshine Act meeting notice, which is an administrative announcement of a board meeting. It concerns credit-union governance procedures and has no substantive regulatory impact on community banks or fintechs.
e2b31fadda63d0e9.title: Sunshine Act Meetings

040b3416ee11195e.plain_english: NCUA finalized administrative updates to its part 749 records preservation and catastrophic act preparedness rules for credit unions, clarifying definitions and removing outdated references. This is a housekeeping rulemaking affecting credit unions' recordkeeping obligations with no substantive new burden.
040b3416ee11195e.title: Records Preservation Program and Appendices-Record Retention Guidelines; Catastrophic Act Preparedness Guidelines
040b3416ee11195e.fr_title: Records Preservation Program and Appendices-Record Retention Guidelines; Catastrophic Act Preparedness Guidelines
040b3416ee11195e.fr_dates_text: This final rule is effective on July 16, 2026.

23e3636a6d8952e5.plain_english: NCUA's interim final rule confirms federal credit unions may charge non-interest fees, including interchange, even when those fees are set jointly with third parties, reinforcing federal preemption over state limits. This affects federal credit unions' fee structures but does not touch banks or fintech/BaaS arrangements.
23e3636a6d8952e5.title: Preemption-Federal Credit Union Non-Interest Charges and Fees
23e3636a6d8952e5.fr_title: Preemption-Federal Credit Union Non-Interest Charges and Fees
23e3636a6d8952e5.fr_dates_text: The interim final rule is effective June 30, 2026. Comments on the interim final rule must be received on or before July 9, 2026.

f5433772722174d2.plain_english: NCUA finalized a rule letting federal credit unions reimburse volunteer officials for dependent care costs incurred during official duties. This is a narrow governance/administrative matter affecting FCUs only, with no impact on banks or fintechs.
f5433772722174d2.title: Dependent Care and Board Member Expense Reimbursement
f5433772722174d2.fr_title: Dependent Care and Board Member Expense Reimbursement
f5433772722174d2.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received by on or before March 27, 2026.

38d620fcc8da5e4d.plain_english: This OMB proposal revises government-wide rules for managing federal grants, cooperative agreements, and financial assistance awards. It concerns grant recipients and federal grant-making agencies, not community bank or fintech supervision, lending, or BSA/AML obligations. No impact on our focus areas.
38d620fcc8da5e4d.title: Regulation for Federal Financial Assistance

2b2a24b70db8fc75.plain_english: NCUA proposes rules implementing the GENIUS Act to allow subsidiaries of federally insured credit unions to issue payment stablecoins, with amendments covering share insurance and tokenized shares. This hits credit unions and their CUSOs wanting to enter stablecoin issuance, establishing a licensing/supervisory framework for a digital-asset activity.
2b2a24b70db8fc75.title: Implementing the Guiding and Establishing National Innovation for U.S. Stablecoins Act for the Issuance of Stablecoins by Entities Subject to the Jurisdiction of the National Credit Union Administration
2b2a24b70db8fc75.fr_title: Implementing the Guiding and Establishing National Innovation for U.S. Stablecoins Act for the Issuance of Stablecoins by Entities Subject to the Jurisdiction of the National Credit Union Administration
2b2a24b70db8fc75.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received by July 17, 2026.

bb5299cb9533b625.plain_english: FinCEN issued a supplemental alert on fuel smuggling and tax evasion schemes tied to Mexican cartels near the southern border, with new red flags and SAR filing guidance. BSA/AML programs should fold these typologies into transaction monitoring and SAR narratives, particularly for institutions serving fuel, energy, or cross-border trade customers.
bb5299cb9533b625.title: FIN-2026-Alert003

394d7df09fecc58a.plain_english: FinCEN proposed CIP requirements under the GENIUS Act, tied to stablecoin/payment stablecoin issuers, introducing new customer identity and BSA/AML obligations. This hits stablecoin issuers and banks/fintechs supporting stablecoin or BaaS arrangements. As an NPRM, scope and specifics remain subject to comment.
394d7df09fecc58a.title: GENIUS CIP NPRM Fact Sheet

1f95cd4f4cdb29d3.plain_english: FinCEN issued an advisory highlighting money laundering and financial-integrity risks connected to non-work-authorized populations and their employers, with red flags focused on payroll and employer-facing accounts. Institutions should tune transaction monitoring and SAR filing to catch these patterns. This is general BSA/AML guidance affecting any institution with employer/payroll-related accounts.
1f95cd4f4cdb29d3.title: FinCEN Advisory FIN-2026-A002

4f7abc0a610a9d9b.plain_english: FinCEN issued an alert on IRGC use of front companies, financial facilitators, and digital asset infrastructure to evade sanctions and launder proceeds. Institutions—especially those touching crypto or cross-border flows—should fold the described red flags into transaction monitoring and SAR filing. It's advisory rather than a new rule, but it heightens BSA/AML detection expectations.
4f7abc0a610a9d9b.title: FIN-2026-Alert002

2ef8035194bdbdc4.plain_english: FinCEN issued a notice flagging expected upticks in human trafficking around the 2026 FIFA World Cup, with red flags and SAR filing guidance. Institutions should fold these typologies into BSA/AML monitoring and staff training, but it imposes no new binding requirements.
2ef8035194bdbdc4.title: FIN-2026-NTC1

c0a1220b6b181b97.plain_english: FinCEN refreshed its Rapid Response Program fact sheet explaining how it helps recover funds stolen via fraud and cyber-enabled crime by coordinating with law enforcement and foreign partners. It's informational for BSA/AML teams and reinforces prompt SAR and fraud reporting, but imposes no new obligations.
c0a1220b6b181b97.title: FinCEN Rapid Response Program Fact Sheet

a23ddbfa27c964aa.plain_english: FinCEN proposed rules under the GENIUS Act imposing AML/CFT and sanctions compliance program requirements on payment stablecoin issuers and related entities. This hits any bank or fintech issuing stablecoins or running BaaS arrangements that touch digital assets, adding new BSA/AML and sanctions program duties. As a proposal, terms may still change before finalization.
a23ddbfa27c964aa.title: Treasury AML/CFT and SCP NPRM Fact Sheet

d9576b86e0059b81.plain_english: FinCEN proposed a major overhaul of AML/CFT program requirements, requiring risk-based programs tied to national AML priorities. Any institution with BSA/AML obligations would need to reassess and potentially restructure its compliance program. This is foundational and worth submitting comment on.
d9576b86e0059b81.title: FinCEN AML/CFT Program NPRM Fact Sheet

3a9af2534aa6c459.plain_english: FinCEN issued an advisory on health care fraud schemes targeting Medicare, Medicaid, and other benefit programs, with red flags and SAR filing guidance. Any institution with a BSA/AML program should incorporate the typologies into transaction monitoring and SAR narratives. No new binding requirements, but examiners will expect awareness.
3a9af2534aa6c459.title: FinCEN Advisory FIN-2026-A001

2f7aaf959ccbdd3c.plain_english: FinCEN publicized its whistleblower program, encouraging tips on fraud-related AML and sanctions violations. It creates no new obligations but signals continued enforcement focus and raises the risk that employees flag BSA/AML gaps. Institutions should verify internal compliance and reporting channels are solid.
2f7aaf959ccbdd3c.title: FinCEN Whistleblower Bulletin: Blow the Whistle on Fraud-Related AML and Sanctions Violations

09d425478c6f912d.plain_english: FinCEN revised its guidance on the voluntary 314(b) information-sharing program, clarifying that participants can share information to detect and prevent fraud (not just money laundering). NCUA is flagging it for BSA/AML programs. No new mandatory requirements—it broadens permitted anti-fraud use cases for those who opt in.
09d425478c6f912d.title: NCUA Highlights Anti-Fraud and Other Updates in FinCEN’s Revised 314(b) Program Guidance

3eee49204f314ab6.plain_english: NCUA placed WeDevelopment Federal Credit Union into conservatorship, taking control to address problems while keeping it operating. This is a single-institution supervisory action affecting that credit union's members; broader industry impact is minimal.
3eee49204f314ab6.title: WeDevelopment Federal Credit Union Conserved

b430065c7d9b0fbb.plain_english: NCUA announced additional funding opportunities under the Community Development Revolving Loan Fund for credit unions. This is a credit-union-specific funding program; relevant to low-income-designated credit unions but not fintechs.
b430065c7d9b0fbb.title: CDRLF Loan Program Offers Additional Opportunities

b839885ec3df30ce.plain_english: NCUA announced $3.47 million in CDRLF grants awarded to credit unions. This is an administrative grant distribution with no rule change or compliance implications.
b839885ec3df30ce.title: NCUA Awards $3.47 Million through Expanded CDRLF Grants

a15502079697297c.plain_english: NCUA raised a commemorative flag for the nation's 250th anniversary. This is a ceremonial/administrative event with no regulatory content.
a15502079697297c.title: NCUA Raises America 250 Flag to Commemorate the Nation's 250th Anniversary

86425d18da48b5df.plain_english: NCUA's board received briefings on regulatory updates, the Share Insurance Fund, and its mid-year budget. This is a routine board meeting summary with no new rules or requirements, though it touches on Share Insurance Fund status relevant to credit unions.
86425d18da48b5df.title: NCUA Board Briefed on Regulatory Updates, the Share Insurance Fund, and the Mid-Year Budget

250773ec6b1d9bc4.plain_english: This is a routine administrative notice announcing an NCUA Board meeting on June 24 with no substantive rule content. Nothing here changes any obligations.
250773ec6b1d9bc4.title: NCUA Board to Hold Meeting on June 24

b9860a9720fbdfb4.plain_english: NCUA published routine Q1 2026 state-level credit union performance statistics. No new requirements—useful as benchmarking data for credit unions but purely informational.
b9860a9720fbdfb4.title: NCUA Releases Q1 2026 State-level Credit Union Data Report

e09132251528cfc0.plain_english: The FDIC issued localized supervisory relief for institutions serving the San Carlos Apache Tribe area affected by storms and flooding. This is a narrow, geography-specific disaster-relief notice with no broad regulatory change relevant to our focus areas.
e09132251528cfc0.title: Supervisory Relief to Help Financial Institutions and Facilitate Recovery for the San Carlos Apache Tribe of Arizona Affected by Severe Storms and Flooding

d72589f8ba89f06e.plain_english: The FDIC proposed new reporting forms and instructions for FDIC-supervised entities issuing permitted payment stablecoins under the GENIUS Act. This creates fresh reporting obligations for any bank or fintech entering stablecoin issuance, and firms weighing a stablecoin/payments strategy should gauge the compliance burden now and comment.
d72589f8ba89f06e.title: GENIUS Act: Proposed Reporting Forms and Instructions for FDIC-Supervised Permitted Payment Stablecoin Issuers
d72589f8ba89f06e.fr_title: Reporting Forms and Instructions Associated With Requirements and Standards for FDIC-Supervised Permitted Payment Stablecoin Issuers
d72589f8ba89f06e.fr_dates_text: Comments must be submitted on or before September 18, 2026.

954a81b2c8fefcab.plain_english: The FDIC issued localized supervisory relief for institutions serving tribal areas in Montana affected by a severe storm. This is a disaster-recovery accommodation limited to a specific geography and event, not a substantive regulatory change.
954a81b2c8fefcab.title: Supervisory Relief to Help Financial Institutions and Facilitate Recovery for the Fort Peck Assiniboine and Sioux Tribes and the Crow Tribe of Montana Affected by Severe Storm and Straight-Line Winds

457b77e1c48f95d5.plain_english: The FDIC is encouraging (not requiring) banks to make use of the voluntary 314(b) information-sharing safe harbor to better detect money laundering and terrorist financing. No new obligations are imposed; it's a nudge to strengthen BSA/AML programs by sharing information with other financial institutions.
457b77e1c48f95d5.title: FDIC Encourages Voluntary Information Sharing Under 314(b) of the USA PATRIOT Act

d94a16ab562d1b97.plain_english: Routine quarterly reminder that FDIC-supervised banks must file Q2 2026 Call Reports per the FFIEC schedule. No new requirements—standard mandatory filing obligation.
d94a16ab562d1b97.title: Consolidated Reports of Condition and Income for Second Quarter 2026

6affdc6ea23c1f59.plain_english: The FDIC issued a proposed rule on disclosure of information, but the summary provides no substantive detail on scope, requirements, or affected parties. Without additional content, it cannot be tied to community bank or fintech focus areas like BaaS, BSA/AML, or consumer lending.
6affdc6ea23c1f59.title: Notice of Proposed Rulemaking on Disclosure of Information

32b7592643d53117.plain_english: The FDIC proposes revising deposit insurance assessment thresholds, rate schedules, and adjustments, which would change what banks pay in FDIC premiums. Direction and magnitude aren't specified in the summary, but any change directly affects community bank cost structures. Comment period details not provided.
32b7592643d53117.title: Notice of Proposed Rulemaking on Assessments Thresholds, Rate Schedules, and Adjustments
32b7592643d53117.fr_title: Assessments Thresholds, Rate Schedules, and Adjustments
32b7592643d53117.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received no later than August 31, 2026.

601f383717e4411b.plain_english: The FDIC proposes to amend rules requiring resolution submissions from covered insured depository institutions, which apply to large banks (generally $50B+ or $100B+ in assets). This falls outside the community bank and fintech threshold we monitor and does not affect institutions under ~$10B.
601f383717e4411b.title: Notice of Proposed Rulemaking to Amend the Rule Requiring Resolution Submissions by Covered Insured Depository Institutions

0c8360bf9bd9ec30.plain_english: OFAC updated a Venezuela-related FAQ clarifying current sanctions interpretations. Institutions with Venezuela-linked customers or transactions should confirm their OFAC screening and BSA/AML programs align; impact is minimal absent such exposure.
0c8360bf9bd9ec30.title: Issuance of Venezuela-related Frequently Asked Question

61c815be5f81aef2.plain_english: OFAC updated its SDN/sanctions list with additions, changes, and removals of Hong Kong-related designations. Compliance teams must refresh screening filters to avoid processing prohibited transactions.
61c815be5f81aef2.title: Hong Kong-related Designations Updates and Removals

494d7795c45200f9.plain_english: OFAC added new non-proliferation and counter-terrorism designations to the SDN list. Any institution processing payments or onboarding customers must update sanctions screening to catch the newly designated parties; failure to screen risks processing prohibited transactions.
494d7795c45200f9.title: Non-Proliferation Designations; Counter Terrorism Designations

18c43a8d13921840.plain_english: OFAC added new Iran-related and counter-terrorism entries to the SDN list and issued a related general license. Any institution with OFAC screening obligations must update filters and block or reject transactions with the newly designated parties. This is routine sanctions-list maintenance requiring prompt screening updates.
18c43a8d13921840.title: Iran-related Designations; Iran-related and Counter Terrorism Designation Update; Issuance of Iran-related General License

987aa092cdcc42ec.plain_english: OFAC added new cyber-related and Cuba SDN designations and issued a Cuba-related FAQ. BSA/AML and compliance teams must update sanctions screening filters against the revised SDN list to avoid processing prohibited transactions.
987aa092cdcc42ec.title: Cyber-related Designations; Cuba Designations; Issuance of Cuba-related Frequently Asked Question

afa68b9e9f5752cd.plain_english: OFAC added new Iran-related and counter-terrorism designations to the SDN list and issued a related general license. Anyone running sanctions screening must update their filters immediately to avoid processing transactions for the newly designated parties. This is routine but time-sensitive OFAC compliance.
afa68b9e9f5752cd.title: Iran-related and Counter Terrorism Designations; Issuance of Iran-related General License

9b37abdfd25c080c.plain_english: OFAC issued a general license under the DRC sanctions program authorizing certain otherwise-prohibited transactions. Sanctions screening teams should review the license terms, but impact is minimal absent DRC-related exposure.
9b37abdfd25c080c.title: Issuance of Democratic Republic of the Congo-related General License

d170e827e18c313d.plain_english: OFAC amended a Russia-related General License and updated related FAQs, adjusting what activity is authorized under the sanctions program. Institutions with cross-border or sanctions-exposed activity should update OFAC screening and BSA/AML procedures to reflect the revised authorizations.
d170e827e18c313d.title: Issuance of Amended Russia-related General License and Frequently Asked Questions

9a9eef218e4370c3.plain_english: OFAC amended an Iran-related general license, changing the scope of authorized activity under Iran sanctions. The summary gives no specifics, so compliance teams should read the license text to determine whether their sanctions screening, transaction monitoring, or customer base is affected. This is a general BSA/AML/sanctions matter, not a fintech-specific development.
9a9eef218e4370c3.title: Issuance of Amended Iran-related General License

3d4b4e9a48618d70.plain_english: OFAC issued its routine annual reminder to file the 2026 Annual Report of Blocked Property. Institutions holding any blocked or frozen sanctioned assets must file; those with no blocked property generally have no obligation. Standard sanctions-compliance housekeeping tied to existing BSA/AML programs.
3d4b4e9a48618d70.title: Reminder to File the 2026 Annual Report of Blocked Property

bc72aedb3e3556b3.plain_english: CSBS announced selected academic papers for its 2026 Community Banking Research Conference. This is an administrative/event announcement with no regulatory requirements or deadlines.
bc72aedb3e3556b3.title: Community Banking Research Conference Announces 2026 Selected Papers

ed7d60eaa1966824.plain_english: This is a CSBS podcast episode discussing the future of state supervision, not a rule, guidance, or enforcement action. It contains no specific requirements, deadlines, or obligations relevant to community banks or fintechs.
ed7d60eaa1966824.title: In the Loop Episode 1 | The Future of State Supervision with Bo Fears and Brandon Milhorn

b2003cd9f0c70fb8.plain_english: CSBS published a press release summarizing community banker sentiment, indicating an optimistic outlook despite some concerns. This is a survey/sentiment report, not a rule, guidance, or requirement affecting compliance obligations.
b2003cd9f0c70fb8.title: Community Bankers Continue Rosy Outlook Despite Concerns

94cebda24e8c9e49.plain_english: This is a press release referencing UBPR (Uniform Bank Performance Report) loan nomenclature and other changes, but the summary provides no substantive details. Without further content, it appears to be a routine reporting/technical update rather than a rule with new compliance obligations.
94cebda24e8c9e49.title: UBPR Loan Nomenclature and Other Changes

98bb84afd26b6120.plain_english: This is an introductory podcast episode from CSBS introducing its hosts, not a regulatory action. It contains no rules, guidance, or requirements relevant to community banks or fintechs.
98bb84afd26b6120.title: In the Loop Episode 0 | Meet Our Hosts, Derek and Kareeme

b50d016765c2b27d.plain_english: CSBS issued a press release commenting on federal capital proposals, but the provided text contains no substantive details. Capital rules of this type typically target larger banks and the summary offers nothing actionable for community banks under $10B or fintechs.
b50d016765c2b27d.title: Agencies’ Capital Proposals Much Advanced but Warrant Further Improvements

2a861804e53eb953.plain_english: A bipartisan group of senators sent a letter to Treasury endorsing state-level stablecoin regulatory frameworks, signaling that stablecoin oversight may be shared between federal and state regulators. This is advocacy, not a rule, but it hints at the future regulatory structure for fintechs and sponsor banks engaged in stablecoin activity. No compliance action is required now.
2a861804e53eb953.title: Bipartisan Senate Letter to U.S. Treasury Supports State Stablecoin Frameworks

e15f45c0ffe599e3.plain_english: CSBS announced a new NMLS Ombudsman appointment. This is a routine personnel/administrative announcement with no regulatory change or compliance obligation for community banks or fintechs.
e15f45c0ffe599e3.title: Idaho’s Erin Van Engelen Named NMLS Ombudsman

4967e0257d012481.plain_english: The FTC finalized an order against a supplement company (TruHeight) for deceptive health claims and fake reviews, requiring $750,000 in payment. This is a consumer-products advertising enforcement matter unrelated to banking, fintech, lending, or BSA/AML.
4967e0257d012481.title: FTC Approves Final Order Against TruHeight for Deceptive and Unsubstantiated Advertising of Supplements for Kids and Teens

77876aa79722cfd7.plain_english: The FTC endorsed an Ohio Supreme Court proposal to weaken the ABA's law school accreditation monopoly for bar admission. This concerns legal education and professional licensing, not banking, fintech, or financial regulation.
77876aa79722cfd7.title: FTC Endorses Ohio Supreme Court Proposal to Weaken ABA’s Law School Accreditation Monopoly

bdf06c90394ec7cd.plain_english: The FTC settled an antitrust case against pharmacy benefit manager Caremark to lower drug costs and improve pharmacy fairness. This concerns healthcare/PBM markets and has no bearing on community banks, fintechs, or their compliance obligations.
bdf06c90394ec7cd.title: FTC Secures Major Settlement with Caremark, Resolving Antitrust Case Against Second Drug Middleman

5e94ca6f2279b4bf.plain_english: The FTC penalized medical device makers Edwards Lifesciences and Genesis $12 million for allegedly structuring an acquisition to avoid Hart-Scott-Rodino antitrust review. This is an antitrust/pre-merger reporting matter in the medical device sector with no bearing on community banks or fintechs.
5e94ca6f2279b4bf.title: FTC Secures $12 Million in Penalties for Pre-Merger Reporting Act Violations

94aec5504815c78b.plain_english: The FTC settled with tenant screening firm RentGrow for $2.25M over FCRA accuracy and disclosure failures. This targets a consumer reporting agency in the tenant screening space, not community banks, fintechs, or BaaS/lending arrangements. It's a reminder of FCRA accuracy obligations but falls outside the defined focus.
94aec5504815c78b.title: RentGrow to Pay $2.25 Million to Settle FTC Allegations the Company Violated the Fair Credit Reporting Act and FTC Act

b7e2764c9e698ff0.plain_english: The FTC and five states settled an antitrust right-to-repair lawsuit against John Deere over restrictions on repairing farm equipment. This concerns manufacturing/repair competition and has no bearing on community bank or fintech operations, BaaS, BSA/AML, or lending obligations.
b7e2764c9e698ff0.title: FTC, States Secure Settlement with Deere & Company, Advancing Farmers’ Right to Repair

8ecddbec9754514f.plain_english: The FTC is distributing over $2.7 million in refunds to consumers harmed by Handy Technologies, a gig-economy home services platform. This is a consumer redress distribution unrelated to community banking, fintech BaaS, lending, or BSA/AML.
8ecddbec9754514f.title: FTC Sends More Than $2.7 Million to Consumers Harmed by Handy Technologies

dbec76e7786c38b2.plain_english: The FTC sent warning letters to companies over allegedly false 'Made in the USA' and 'Made in Texas' claims. This concerns product origin labeling and consumer goods marketing, not banking or fintech operations.
dbec76e7786c38b2.title: FTC Warns Companies Making Questionable ‘Made in the USA’ Claims

31fd9e1bf8d4f543.plain_english: The FTC finalized a settlement against Publishing.com over deceptive earnings claims for self-publishing products. This is a consumer-protection enforcement action against an unrelated company and does not touch community banking, fintech, BaaS, or lending obligations.
31fd9e1bf8d4f543.title: FTC Approves Final Order Against Publishing.com, Settling Allegations It Misled Consumers

0861d66b027015a6.plain_english: The FTC and DOJ released their annual Hart-Scott-Rodino report on merger filings under antitrust premerger notification rules. This is a routine statistical/administrative report on merger enforcement activity and does not impose requirements on community banks or fintechs.
0861d66b027015a6.title: FTC and DOJ Issue Fiscal Year 2025 Hart-Scott-Rodino Annual Report

2c1f2a0a4dd16c62.plain_english: The CFTC is sunsetting routine large trader reporting requirements for physical commodity swaps. This affects derivatives market participants and swap dealers, not community banks or fintechs in the areas we monitor.
2c1f2a0a4dd16c62.title: CFTC Sunsets Routine Large Trader Reporting Requirements for Physical Commodity Swaps , 9269-26

f105838699e22ac0.plain_english: The CFTC announced a meeting of its Agricultural Advisory Committee on July 29 in Washington. This is a routine administrative meeting notice with no relevance to community banking or fintech regulation.
f105838699e22ac0.title: Chairman Selig Announces CFTC Agricultural Advisory Committee to Meet July 29 in Washington , 9268-26

98f1cdcbfc55b56c.plain_english: The CFTC stayed a KalshiEX rule change and used emergency authority to order fulfillment of pending trades on the prediction/event-contract exchange. This concerns a CFTC-regulated derivatives/event-market platform, not community banks, sponsor-bank arrangements, payments, or the other fintech scopes we track.
98f1cdcbfc55b56c.title: CFTC Stays KalshiEX Rule Change and Exercises Emergency Authority to Order Fulfillment of Pending Trades , 9267-26

5cfa5fae8f09cc4c.plain_english: The CFTC finalized amendments to margin requirements for uncleared swaps. This affects swap dealers and major swap participants in derivatives markets, not community banks or fintechs within our focus areas.
5cfa5fae8f09cc4c.title: CFTC Approves Final Rule Amending Margin Requirements for Uncleared Swaps , 9266-26

e8f17b0a5ccb42c1.plain_english: The CFTC is staying a self-certified contract enabling 24/7 trading of crude oil futures. This is a commodity derivatives market matter with no bearing on community banks, fintechs, or the payments/BaaS/lending areas we track.
e8f17b0a5ccb42c1.title: CFTC to Stay Self-Certified Contract on 24/7 Trading for Crude Oil Futures , 9265-26

f5ec2f3e3d2fc8fc.plain_english: The CFTC charged a North Carolina commodity pool operator and his company with fraud. This is a routine commodity fraud enforcement action unrelated to community banks, fintechs, or bank-fintech partnerships.
f5ec2f3e3d2fc8fc.title: CFTC Charges North Carolina Commodity Pool Operator and His Company with Fraud , 9264-26

fbe070d9480161b0.plain_english: The CFTC fined two foreign firms $2.5 million for illegal off-exchange transactions with U.S. customers. This is a derivatives enforcement matter with no bearing on community bank or fintech operations in our scope.
fbe070d9480161b0.title: CFTC Orders Two Foreign Firms to Pay $2.5 Million for Illegal Off-Exchange Transactions with U.S. Customers , 9263-26

b4b34d48593fb43b.plain_english: The CFTC and SEC are seeking comment on harmonizing portfolio margining frameworks for derivatives and securities. This is a capital markets/derivatives clearing matter that does not touch community bank or fintech operations in our focus areas.
b4b34d48593fb43b.title: CFTC, SEC Seek Public Comment on the Harmonization of Portfolio Margining Frameworks , 9262-26

2b532dfc0c85766b.plain_english: The CFTC is proposing data reporting requirements for certain event contracts (prediction-market style derivatives). This is a derivatives market data matter that does not touch community bank or fintech operations within our scope.
2b532dfc0c85766b.title: CFTC Seeks Public Comment on Notice of Proposed Rulemaking Concerning Data Reporting Requirements for Certain Event Contracts , 9261-26

d725e20631d2772a.plain_english: The CFTC filed suit against Kentucky in a jurisdictional dispute over the CFTC's exclusive authority. The summary provides no detail tying it to derivatives, crypto, or any topic within our focus, and it does not affect community banks or fintech operations as described.
d725e20631d2772a.title: CFTC Sues Kentucky to Prevent Violation of CFTC’s Exclusive Jurisdiction , 9260-26

32eb57649c40ae36.plain_english: The federal banking agencies removed additional references to reputation risk from interagency supervisory documents, continuing their earlier effort to eliminate reputation risk as a supervisory basis. This is a general supervisory shift meant to keep examiner decisions tied to material financial risk rather than political, religious, or lawful-business considerations. It may reduce debanking pressure, which is relevant to banks serving controversial or fintech-adjacent clients, but it imposes no new requirements.
32eb57649c40ae36.title: Agencies Remove References to Reputation Risk in Interagency Documents

e880efdaaf31a69b.plain_english: Routine monthly FDIC publication of April 2026 enforcement orders against a handful of banks and individuals, updated only to clarify respondent names. No new rules, standards, or fintech-related matters involved.
e880efdaaf31a69b.title: Updated: FDIC Publishes Enforcement Orders for April 2026

b6f41c63c1fa7629.plain_english: The FDIC published its routine quarterly lists of institutions scheduled for CRA examinations in Q3 and Q4 2026. This is a standard administrative notice with no new requirements.
b6f41c63c1fa7629.title: FDIC Issues CRA Examination Schedules for Third Quarter 2026 and Fourth Quarter 2026

4533608f9474b55b.plain_english: The FDIC published its monthly list of April 2026 enforcement actions, including a consent order, a terminated consent order, a notice of charges against Herring Bank, and an adjudicated decision. These are routine institution-specific actions with no fintech or BaaS angle and no broad new requirements.
4533608f9474b55b.title: FDIC Publishes Enforcement Orders for April 2026

101c010760ecd4cf.plain_english: The FDIC's Q1 2026 Quarterly Banking Profile reports industry net income of $80.5B and ROA of 1.26%, with community bank net income up 3.9% and generally favorable asset quality aside from elevated CRE and consumer delinquencies. This is a backward-looking statistical summary with no rule changes or new obligations.
101c010760ecd4cf.title: FDIC-Insured Institutions Reported Return on Assets of 1.26 Percent and Net Income of $80.5 Billion in First Quarter 2026

da7bb43ec6c681dc.plain_english: The FDIC and Fed published resolution plan (living will) feedback letters for the eight largest domestic banks and 56 foreign banking organizations, finding no shortcomings or deficiencies. This concerns only the largest, most complex institutions and has no bearing on community banks or fintechs.
da7bb43ec6c681dc.title: Agencies Publish Resolution Plan Feedback Letters for Certain Domestic and Foreign Banking Organizations

e327d151a07f036b.plain_english: The FDIC and Fed approved feedback letters on 2025 resolution plans for GSIBs and large foreign firms, and signaled upcoming amendments to the IDI Rule and Title I resolution planning reforms. This applies to large, systemically important institutions, not community banks under $10B or fintechs.
e327d151a07f036b.title: Statement by Chairman Travis Hill on Title I Feedback Letters and Resolution-Related Reforms

20d55f3259bf4d42.plain_english: The FDIC proposed BSA/AML and sanctions compliance rules for permitted payment stablecoin issuers (PPSIs) that are subsidiaries of FDIC-supervised state nonmember banks and savings associations, implementing the GENIUS Act. Issuers would need AML/CFT and OFAC programs aligned with FinCEN requirements, with FDIC supervision and enforcement. Relevant to any community bank pursuing a stablecoin-issuing subsidiary.
20d55f3259bf4d42.effective_or_comment_date: Comments due 60 days after Federal Register publication
20d55f3259bf4d42.title: Notice of Proposed Rulemaking to Establish Bank Secrecy Act and Sanctions Compliance Standards for FDIC-Supervised Permitted Payment Stablecoin Issuers
20d55f3259bf4d42.fr_title: Bank Secrecy Act and Sanctions Compliance Standards for FDIC-Supervised Permitted Payment Stablecoin Issuers
20d55f3259bf4d42.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received by the FDIC no later than August 4, 2026.

a9be7d9db852f768.plain_english: The FFIEC proposed the first major overhaul of the CAMELS supervisory rating system since 1996, shifting emphasis away from risk-management process toward the fundamental financial risks that materially affect a bank's condition. This affects how examiners rate all supervised banks, including community banks; internal controls and risk management remain relevant but become secondary to financial fundamentals.
a9be7d9db852f768.title: Statement by Chairman Travis Hill on the Proposal to Revise the CAMELS Rating System

cf69cd534d1f0362.plain_english: The FDIC posted the public sections of triennial informational filings (resolution-related) for six large insured depository institutions. This concerns large IDIs and their resolution planning, not community banks or fintechs.
cf69cd534d1f0362.title: FDIC Releases Public Sections of Informational Filings for Six Insured Depository Institutions

c4dd607824dc6c02.plain_english: The FDIC approved deposit insurance for Stellantis Bank USA, a Utah-chartered industrial bank (ILC) that will focus on auto financing via purchase of retail installment contracts from Stellantis dealers, funded largely by affiliate, brokered and listing-service deposits plus consumer/business deposits online. This continues the trend of FDIC approving ILC charters for commercial parents, relevant as a competitive and charter-strategy signal but not a rule change.
c4dd607824dc6c02.title: FDIC Approves the Deposit Insurance Application for Stellantis Bank USA, Salt Lake City, Utah

f5ff5d45ba0e8111.plain_english: The FDIC published a staff research study analyzing transaction-level deposit flows at SVB, Signature, and First Republic before their 2023 failures, finding uninsured depositors were far more likely to run than insured retail depositors. It's a retrospective analytical study, not a rule or requirement, though it may inform future liquidity and deposit-behavior supervision.
f5ff5d45ba0e8111.title: FDIC Releases Staff Study of Deposit Flows at Three Failed Banks in Spring 2023

adaf5b279e33bdee.plain_english: A $288M Georgia community bank failed and was closed by state regulators, with Anchor Bank assuming its insured deposits and certain assets. This is a routine FDIC receivership announcement with no rule change or fintech angle; roughly $27M in uninsured deposits were at risk.
adaf5b279e33bdee.title: Anchor Bank Assumes Insured Deposits of Community Bank and Trust - West Georgia, LaGrange, Georgia

4ff9807673386a89.plain_english: Nine agencies issued a joint final rule under the Financial Data Transparency Act establishing common data standards to make regulatory data collections interoperable. It affects how regulated entities, including community banks, format and report certain information, but implementation will flow through subsequent agency-specific rulemakings rather than immediate reporting changes.
4ff9807673386a89.title: Joint Rule Establishing Data Standards under the Financial Data Transparency Act of 2022
4ff9807673386a89.fr_title: Financial Data Transparency Act Joint Data Standards
4ff9807673386a89.fr_dates_text: Effective date: The joint rule is effective on October 1, 2026. At the effective date, the joint rule will not change any reporting requirements without further action by the agencies.

199dac7a7ff10804.plain_english: The Fed issued an enforcement action against an individual employee of Bank of Eufaula and its holding company. This is a personnel-specific action with no broader rule or industry-wide implication.
199dac7a7ff10804.title: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement action with employee of Bank of Eufaula and S N B Bancshares, Inc.

f25a1b5a3a7a2093.plain_english: The Fed terminated its enforcement action against Jiko Group, a fintech that operates a bank-fintech model. The termination signals the firm resolved the underlying supervisory concerns, but the action itself carries no new obligations for others.
f25a1b5a3a7a2093.title: Federal Reserve Board announces termination of enforcement action with Jiko Group, Inc.

66958339bcc7a6ea.plain_english: The Fed released annual stress test results confirming large banks can withstand a severe recession. This applies to large banks well above the community bank threshold and has no direct bearing on community banks or fintechs.
66958339bcc7a6ea.title: Federal Reserve Board's annual bank stress test confirms that large banks are well positioned to weather a severe recession and able to continue to lend to households and businesses

a60488f909842790.plain_english: The Federal Reserve issued a memorial notice on the death of former Fed Chairman Alan Greenspan. This carries no regulatory or compliance implications.
a60488f909842790.title: Federal Reserve notes with deep sadness the passing of Alan Greenspan

13d0e1c7175adb95.plain_english: The Fed issued an enforcement action against a former employee of Bank of Eufaula and its holding company. This is an individual personnel-level matter with no broader rulemaking or industry impact.
13d0e1c7175adb95.title: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement action with former employee of Bank of Eufaula and S N B Bancshares, Inc.

47c281cd5c37cf30.plain_english: The Fed issued an enforcement action against a former individual employee of M&T Bank. This is a personnel-level enforcement matter with no broader rule change or industry-wide implications.
47c281cd5c37cf30.title: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement action with former employee of Manufacturers and Traders Trust Company

f0a2125068f6998a.plain_english: The Fed proposes requiring certain payment stablecoin issuers to maintain an effective customer identification program (CIP). This directly targets stablecoin issuers, adding BSA/AML-style KYC obligations to the digital-asset payments space.
f0a2125068f6998a.title: Federal Reserve Board requests comment on proposal to require certain payment stablecoin issuers to maintain an effective customer identification program

bbe9f0695d8df486.plain_english: The FOMC issued its routine monetary policy statement setting the federal funds rate stance. This is a macroeconomic policy announcement, not a supervisory or regulatory change affecting bank or fintech compliance obligations.
bbe9f0695d8df486.title: Federal Reserve issues FOMC statement

eeda99166431b34f.plain_english: The FOMC released its quarterly economic projections following the June meeting. This is macroeconomic forecasting, not a regulatory change or compliance obligation.
eeda99166431b34f.title: Federal Reserve Board and Federal Open Market Committee release economic projections from the June 16-17 FOMC meeting

a18cb1a5b8d2a48b.plain_english: The Fed finalized a rule adopting standardized data formats for certain regulatory information collections, part of the Financial Data Transparency Act implementation. It affects entities that file the covered collections but is an administrative data-standardization measure, not a substantive change to obligations.
a18cb1a5b8d2a48b.title: Federal Reserve Board announces final rule that establishes data standards for certain information collections

95d5b5a3e9331ba4.plain_english: The Fed granted an exemption allowing banks to obtain TIN information from a third party rather than directly from the customer under the CIP rule. This is significant for BaaS/sponsor-bank arrangements where a fintech partner collects customer data, easing onboarding compliance friction. Banks relying on the exemption should confirm scope and conditions apply to their partnership model.
95d5b5a3e9331ba4.title: SR 25-2

f8c62f0c8624223b.plain_english: Interagency statement provides examples of risk-management practices banks can use to identify, prevent and respond to elder financial exploitation, including governance, training, transaction monitoring and reporting. It applies broadly to supervised institutions and is non-binding guidance rather than a new rule.
f8c62f0c8624223b.title: CA 24-6 / SR 24-8

aefdeda07c037033.plain_english: Interagency statement providing supervised institutions with resources and risk-management approaches to identify, prevent and respond to elder financial exploitation. Applies broadly to banks; it's general consumer-protection/BSA-adjacent guidance rather than a fintech-specific requirement.
aefdeda07c037033.title: SR 24-8 / CA 24-6

326e3ba0ee969e7f.plain_english: The Fed announced the FFIEC is sunsetting its Cybersecurity Assessment Tool (CAT), which banks had voluntarily used to gauge cyber risk and preparedness. Institutions that relied on the CAT should transition to other frameworks (e.g., NIST CSF, CISA resources). This is a general cybersecurity/safety-and-soundness notice affecting all supervised banks.
326e3ba0ee969e7f.title: SR 24-7

350519e44b0958e3.plain_english: The Fed adopted the updated FFIEC IT Examination Handbook booklet on Development, Acquisition, and Maintenance, setting supervisory expectations for how institutions build, buy, and maintain IT systems and manage related risks including third-party software. This is general IT risk-management guidance for supervised banks, not a fintech-specific rule.
350519e44b0958e3.title: SR 24-6

fdb0d6777233e153.plain_english: The CFPB settled its longstanding lawsuit against pawnshop lender FirstCash and 19 subsidiaries over alleged Military Lending Act violations, with a stipulated final judgment awaiting court entry.
