This is a consumer-lending enforcement matter involving MLA rate caps and disclosures for servicemembers, relevant as a reminder to any consumer lender extending credit to military borrowers.
fdb0d6777233e153.title: CFPB Reaches Settlement with FirstCash, Inc. and Its Subsidiaries for Military Lending Act Violations

f7c71516a8199c2c.plain_english: The CFPB amended its January 2025 consent order against Wise, an international remittance/money-transfer fintech, over deceptive fee advertising and improper disclosure of exchange rates and costs. This is a payments/money-transmission enforcement matter signaling continued scrutiny of remittance fee and disclosure practices under Reg E's remittance rule.
f7c71516a8199c2c.title: CFPB Amends Wise Order for Remittance Practices

dc45e93102ee0b91.plain_english: The CFPB says it will not prioritize enforcement based on its May 2024 interpretive rule treating BNPL 'pay-in-four' products as credit cards subject to Regulation Z. This effectively deprioritizes enforcement pressure on BNPL providers and bank partners over TILA/Reg Z disclosure and dispute obligations, though the underlying rule is not formally rescinded.
dc45e93102ee0b91.title: CFPB Announcement Regarding Enforcement Actions Related to Buy Now, Pay Later Loans

ced1a8953d6d0cfb.plain_english: The CFPB says it will not prioritize enforcement or supervision against entities currently outside the litigation stay in Texas Bankers Association v. CFPB (the 1071 small-business lending data rule case). This is a de-prioritization signal on 1071 compliance rather than a rule change; the underlying obligations remain unless the courts or Bureau formally alter them.
ced1a8953d6d0cfb.title: CFPB Keeps Its Enforcement and Supervision Resources Focused on Pressing Threats to Consumers

aa7f260ec223291e.plain_english: The CFPB says it will not prioritize enforcement or supervision against nonbank entities that miss upcoming deadlines to register under the nonbank covered persons order registry. This offers de facto relief to nonbank lenders and other covered fintechs subject to agency or court orders, though the underlying rule remains on the books.
aa7f260ec223291e.title: CFPB Offers Regulatory Relief From Registration Requirements for Small Loan Providers

cd2fbb2d2525daff.plain_english: The CFPB released the 2024 loan-level HMDA mortgage lending data, modified to protect consumer privacy. This is a routine data publication with no new compliance obligations.
cd2fbb2d2525daff.title: 2024 HMDA Data on Mortgage Lending Now Available

ec5da26b21ee006c.plain_english: The CFPB signaled it will redirect enforcement and supervision away from small loan providers, prioritizing threats to servicemembers and veterans. This suggests reduced supervisory pressure on smaller consumer lenders, though it doesn't change any underlying rules.
ec5da26b21ee006c.title: CFPB Offers Regulatory Relief for Small Loan Providers

e826eaa936463af1.plain_english: The CFPB moved to vacate its own redlining/fair-lending enforcement action against mortgage lender Townstone. This is a fair-lending enforcement matter and a signal of shifting CFPB enforcement posture, not a fintech or BaaS issue.
e826eaa936463af1.title: CFPB Seeks to Vacate Abusive, Unjust Case Against Townstone

64a7f7d91c1b9ffc.plain_english: The President designated Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent as Acting Director of the CFPB. This is a leadership/personnel announcement with no immediate substantive rule or requirement, though it may signal shifts in CFPB priorities.
64a7f7d91c1b9ffc.title: Statement on Designation of Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent as Acting Director of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau

ffdf7498ef5acafc.plain_english: The CFPB issued its routine annual inflation adjustments to Regulation Z dollar thresholds for credit card penalty fees, HOEPA points-and-fees triggers, and qualified mortgage points-and-fees limits. Any lender offering credit cards or covered mortgages must update systems to the new figures. This is standard yearly recalibration, not a substantive policy shift.
ffdf7498ef5acafc.title: Truth in Lending (Regulation Z) Annual Threshold Adjustments (Credit Cards, HOEPA, and Qualified Mortgages)
ffdf7498ef5acafc.fr_title: Truth in Lending (Regulation Z) Annual Threshold Adjustments (Credit Cards, HOEPA, and Qualified Mortgages)
ffdf7498ef5acafc.fr_dates_text: This final rule is effective January 1, 2026.

d7e1acfdc2f88db0.plain_english: The CFPB is rescinding its rule that required certain nonbank covered persons to register final public enforcement orders in a CFPB registry. This eliminates the registration/reporting burden on nonbank entities, including many fintech lenders and other consumer-finance firms that would have been covered.
d7e1acfdc2f88db0.title: Registry of Nonbank Covered Persons Subject to Certain Agency and Court Orders; Rescission
d7e1acfdc2f88db0.fr_title: Registry of Nonbank Covered Persons Subject to Certain Agency and Court Orders; Rescission
d7e1acfdc2f88db0.fr_dates_text: This rule is effective on October 29, 2025.

18e8afbe9b800a36.plain_english: The CFPB extended compliance dates for the Section 1071 small business lending data collection rule under Regulation B. Lenders subject to the rule, including community banks that make small business loans, get more time to build out data collection and reporting systems.
18e8afbe9b800a36.title: Small Business Lending under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act (Regulation B); Extension of Compliance Dates
18e8afbe9b800a36.fr_title: Small Business Lending Under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act (Regulation B); Extension of Compliance Dates
18e8afbe9b800a36.fr_dates_text: This final rule is effective December 1, 2025.

ae0b55a066d8c6f5.plain_english: The CFPB is rescinding an internal procedural rule that specified how and when it issues rules. This is an administrative/housekeeping change with no substantive compliance impact on banks or fintechs.
ae0b55a066d8c6f5.title: Procedure Related to Rulemaking; Rescission

c1d9e6b554438239.plain_english: The CFPB finalized amendments to Regulation V (FCRA) restricting creditors from using medical information in credit decisions and limiting how consumer reporting agencies furnish medical debt data. Any lender that considers medical information in underwriting—including consumer and marketplace lenders—must adjust its credit evaluation practices.
c1d9e6b554438239.title: Prohibition on Creditors and Consumer Reporting Agencies Concerning Medical Information (Regulation V)
c1d9e6b554438239.fr_title: Prohibition on Creditors and Consumer Reporting Agencies Concerning Medical Information (Regulation V)
c1d9e6b554438239.fr_dates_text: This final rule is effective March 17, 2024.

631b98945bd14f56.plain_english: The CFPB made its annual inflation adjustment to the maximum civil penalty amounts it can impose. This is a routine administrative update that raises potential exposure slightly for any entity subject to CFPB enforcement, but imposes no new compliance obligations.
631b98945bd14f56.title: Civil Penalty Inflation Adjustments
631b98945bd14f56.fr_dates_text: This final rule is effective January 15, 2025.

cd752757e111c557.plain_english: The CFPB made its annual inflation adjustment to the HMDA asset-size exemption threshold, which determines whether depository institutions must report mortgage data under Regulation C. Smaller banks and credit unions below the updated threshold remain exempt from HMDA reporting. This is a routine yearly recalibration with minimal operational impact.
cd752757e111c557.title: Home Mortgage Disclosure (Regulation C) Adjustment to Asset-Size Exemption Threshold
cd752757e111c557.fr_title: Home Mortgage Disclosure (Regulation C) Adjustment to Asset-Size Exemption Threshold
cd752757e111c557.fr_dates_text: This rule is effective on January 7, 2026.

364cca5d8e593000.plain_english: The CFPB made its routine annual inflation adjustment to the asset-size thresholds that exempt smaller creditors from the escrow requirement on higher-priced mortgage loans. Community banks near the $2 billion (and EGRRCPA's $10 billion) thresholds should confirm their exemption status, but this is a mechanical CPI-based update, not a substantive change.
364cca5d8e593000.title: Truth in Lending Act (Regulation Z) Adjustment to Asset-Size Exemption Threshold
364cca5d8e593000.fr_title: Truth in Lending Act (Regulation Z) Adjustment to Asset-Size Exemption Threshold
364cca5d8e593000.fr_dates_text: This rule is effective on January 7, 2026.

e6ce505742e22847.plain_english: The CFPB finalized amendments to Regulation Z applying TILA disclosure and ability-to-repay requirements to residential PACE (Property Assessed Clean Energy) financing. This affects lenders and servicers involved in PACE transactions, treating them more like traditional consumer credit. It's a niche consumer-lending rule rather than a fintech-specific matter.
e6ce505742e22847.title: Residential Property Assessed Clean Energy Financing (Regulation Z)
e6ce505742e22847.fr_title: Residential Property Assessed Clean Energy Financing (Regulation Z)
e6ce505742e22847.fr_dates_text: This final rule is effective March 1, 2026.

0c3ffcfe8c5676b6.plain_english: The CFPB finalized rules treating overdraft as a credit product subject to Truth in Lending-style protections, but the rule applies only to very large financial institutions (those with over $10B in assets). Community banks under $10B and most fintechs fall outside its scope.
0c3ffcfe8c5676b6.title: Overdraft Lending: Very Large Financial Institutions Final Rule

115006c4ce776b50.plain_english: The CFPB finalized a rule bringing large nonbank operators of digital payment apps and wallets (funds transfer apps and digital wallets) under its supervisory and examination authority. This directly hits big fintech payment providers, subjecting them to bank-like CFPB exams on privacy, error resolution, and consumer protection.
115006c4ce776b50.title: Defining Larger Participants of a Market for General-Use Digital Consumer Payment Applications
115006c4ce776b50.fr_title: Defining Larger Participants of a Market for General-Use Digital Consumer Payment Applications

e5ce168b0e489768.plain_english: The CFPB made its routine annual inflation adjustment to the maximum charge consumers can be charged for a file disclosure under Regulation V (FCRA). Any lender or consumer reporting user relying on this threshold should update the figure, but it's a ministerial change with no substantive new obligations.
e5ce168b0e489768.title: Fair Credit Reporting Act Disclosures Annual Adjustment
e5ce168b0e489768.fr_dates_text: This final rule is effective January 1, 2026.

aa8e6fc822c94cef.plain_english: The CFPB finalized its Section 1033 open banking rule, requiring covered data providers to give consumers and authorized third parties access to their personal financial data via standardized interfaces. This directly affects data-access fintechs, aggregators, and banks that must build compliant APIs and manage third-party access. Compliance timelines are tiered by institution size, with smaller entities getting later deadlines.
aa8e6fc822c94cef.title: Required Rulemaking on Personal Financial Data Rights
aa8e6fc822c94cef.fr_title: Required Rulemaking on Personal Financial Data Rights
aa8e6fc822c94cef.fr_dates_text: This final rule is effective January 17, 2025.

a7d4efb1be5e3f41.plain_english: The OCC, FDIC, and NCUA propose requiring banks and credit unions to maintain 'effective, risk-based' AML/CFT programs aligned with FinCEN's concurrent AML Act rulemaking, including mandatory risk assessments and greater FinCEN supervisory involvement. This hits all supervised institutions' BSA/AML compliance frameworks, requiring program updates to demonstrate risk-based prioritization.
a7d4efb1be5e3f41.title: Agencies Request Comment on Anti-Money Laundering/Countering the Financing of Terrorism Proposed Rule
a7d4efb1be5e3f41.fr_title: Anti-Money Laundering and Countering the Financing of Terrorism Programs
a7d4efb1be5e3f41.fr_dates_text: Written comments may be submitted on or before June 9, 2026.

57128ccec518667c.plain_english: FinCEN issued a Geographic Targeting Order requiring banks and money transmitters in Hennepin and Ramsey Counties, Minnesota to retain and report records of certain payments of $3,000 or more. Money transmitters and MSBs operating in those counties face immediate recordkeeping and reporting obligations for covered transactions.
57128ccec518667c.title: Geographic Targeting Order Imposing Recordkeeping and Reporting Requirements on Certain Financial Institutions in Minnesota

2a21fb52ec2dfe73.plain_english: FinCEN proposes delaying the AML/CFT program and SAR filing requirements for registered and exempt reporting investment advisers from January 1, 2026 to January 1, 2028. This affects investment advisers, not community banks or fintechs in our BaaS/payments/lending scope.
2a21fb52ec2dfe73.title: Delaying the Effective Date of the Anti-Money Laundering/Countering the Financing of Terrorism Program and Suspicious Activity Report Filing Requirements for Registered Investment Advisers and Exempt Reporting Advisers

fab0dfb14ef502ac.plain_english: Routine Paperwork Reduction Act notice seeking comment on renewing, without change, BSA information collection requirements tied to special measures against entities of primary money laundering concern. No substantive change to obligations.
fab0dfb14ef502ac.title: Agency Information Collection Activities; Proposed Renewal; Comment Request: Renewal Without Change of Information Collection Requirements in Connection With the Imposition of Special Measures

ba246471d09109e4.plain_english: FinCEN proposes a Section 311 special measure targeting ten Mexican gambling establishments as a primary money laundering concern, barring US financial institutions from processing correspondent-account transactions involving them and requiring special due diligence to screen for such activity. This is a narrow BSA/AML obligation affecting institutions with foreign correspondent relationships; most community banks and fintechs without such exposure will have limited practical impact but should ensure screening covers the named entities.
ba246471d09109e4.title: Proposal of Special Measure Regarding Transactions Involving Ten Mexican Gambling Establishments as a Class of Transactions of Primary Money Laundering Concern
ba246471d09109e4.fr_title: Proposal of Special Measure Regarding Transactions Involving Ten Mexican Gambling Establishments as a Class of Transactions of Primary Money Laundering Concern
ba246471d09109e4.fr_dates_text: Written comments on the notice of proposed rulemaking must be submitted on or before December 17, 2025.

f189bec72abca98e.plain_english: FinCEN issued a Section 311 final rule barring US financial institutions from opening or maintaining correspondent accounts for Cambodia-based Huione Group, and requiring special due diligence on foreign correspondent accounts to block transactions touching Huione. Huione is tied to major crypto/online scam money laundering, so institutions with payments, crypto, or correspondent-banking exposure need to screen for and reject Huione-linked flows.
f189bec72abca98e.title: Imposition of Special Measure Regarding Huione Group, as a Foreign Financial Institution of Primary Money Laundering Concern

7222847729251ffa.plain_english: FinCEN issued a special-measure order under the Fentanyl Sanctions/FEND Off Fentanyl authority prohibiting covered US financial institutions from certain funds transmittals involving Mexico's Intercam Banco, deemed a primary money-laundering concern tied to opioid trafficking. Anyone processing flows that could touch Huione needs to update BSA/AML controls and transaction monitoring.

7279361822695b5e.plain_english: FinCEN's interim final rule guts CTA beneficial ownership reporting: domestic reporting companies are now fully exempt, and foreign reporting companies need not report BOI of any U.S. person owners. Only foreign entities must still file, with a 30-day deadline extension. This dramatically narrows the BOI dataset banks may have expected to leverage for CDD/onboarding, though it does not change banks' own BSA/AML obligations.

6f457d375634890a.plain_english: FinCEN issued a Geographic Targeting Order requiring certain MSBs in specified southwest border areas to report and retain records for cash transactions above $200 (down from the usual $10,000 CTR threshold) and to verify customer identity. 765e2f3459a36567.plain_english: Routine PRA paperwork-burden renewal notice for existing BSA administrative-ruling procedures, with no substantive change. It's an administrative comment request that imposes no new obligations.
765e2f3459a36567.title: Agency Information Collection Activities; Proposed Renewal; Comment Request; Renewal Without Change of Administrative Rulings Regulations

087f81da27ccfe7f.plain_english: Routine Paperwork Reduction Act notice seeking comment on renewing, without change, an existing information collection tied to a 2017 special measure against Bank of Dandong. No substantive change to obligations.
087f81da27ccfe7f.title: Agency Information Collection Activities; Proposed Renewal; Comment Request; Renewal Without Change of Information Collection Requirements in Connection With the Imposition of a Special Measure Against Bank of Dandong as a Financial Institution of Primary Money Laundering Concern

0b1eada4b23f7390.plain_english: FinCEN is renewing, without change, a standard Paperwork Reduction Act clearance to collect voluntary feedback on its service delivery. This is a routine administrative notice with no substantive compliance impact.
0b1eada4b23f7390.title: Agency Information Collection Activities: Proposed Renewal; Comment Request; Renewal Without Change of the Generic Clearance for the Collection of Qualitative Feedback on Agency Service Delivery

5e9c60314860337c.plain_english: FinCEN is seeking comment on a routine Paperwork Reduction Act renewal, without change, of information collection requirements tied to the existing Iran special measure. This is purely administrative and imposes no new obligations.
5e9c60314860337c.title: Agency Information Collection Activities; Proposed Renewal; Comment Request: Renewal Without Change of Information Collection Requirements in Connection With the Imposition of the Fifth Special Measure Against the Islamic Republic of Iran as a Jurisdiction of Primary Money Laundering Concern

36481806092ccc07.plain_english: Routine PRA paperwork renewal notice for an existing special measure against Commercial Bank of Syria, with no substantive change. This is an administrative comment request and imposes no new obligations.
36481806092ccc07.title: Agency Information Collection Activities; Proposed Renewal; Comment Request: Renewal Without Change of Information Collection Requirements in Connection With the Imposition of a Special Measure Against Commercial Bank of Syria, Including Its Subsidiary, Syrian Lebanese Commercial Bank, as a Financial Institution of Primary Money Laundering Concern

78f7385dc02053e7.plain_english: FinCEN issued a special-measure order barring covered financial institutions from transmitting funds involving Bitzlato, a foreign crypto exchange deemed a primary money laundering concern tied to Russian illicit finance. Any bank or fintech (including MSBs and crypto-touching institutions) must block transactions connected to Bitzlato. It's a crypto/virtual-currency BSA/AML action requiring immediate transaction screening and controls.
78f7385dc02053e7.title: Imposition of Special Measure Prohibiting the Transmittal of Funds Involving Bitzlato
78f7385dc02053e7.fr_title: Imposition of Special Measure Prohibiting the Transmittal of Funds Involving Bitzlato

27146a89c4d697e8.plain_english: FinCEN is seeking comment under the Paperwork Reduction Act on the application form for individuals to voluntarily obtain a FinCEN identifier under the Beneficial Ownership Information reporting rule. This is a routine administrative information-collection notice, not a substantive rule change.
27146a89c4d697e8.title: Agency Information Collection Activities; Proposed Collection; Comment Request; Individual FinCEN Identifiers

f9265a7feaf604b0.plain_english: FinCEN is seeking comment on the BOI report form used to collect beneficial ownership information under the Corporate Transparency Act reporting rule finalized in September 2022. This is a routine Paperwork Reduction Act notice about the collection instrument, not a substantive change to the reporting requirements. Relevant to BSA/AML compliance teams tracking CTA implementation but low-impact.
f9265a7feaf604b0.title: Agency Information Collection Activities; Proposed Collection; Comment Request; Beneficial Ownership Information Reports
f9265a7feaf604b0.fr_title: Agency Information Collection Activities; Proposed Collection; Comment Request; Beneficial Ownership Information Reports
f9265a7feaf604b0.fr_dates_text: Written comments are welcome and must be received on or before March 20, 2023.

88ecd4b22891f7bf.plain_english: FinCEN proposed rules governing who can access the beneficial ownership information reported under the Corporate Transparency Act and the security/confidentiality protocols that apply to each recipient category, including financial institutions using BOI for CDD compliance. It also specifies how reporting companies can use FinCEN identifiers for entities. Banks that access BOI to satisfy customer due diligence would face strict use, security, and oversight requirements.
88ecd4b22891f7bf.title: Beneficial Ownership Information Access and Safeguards, and Use of FinCEN Identifiers for Entities
88ecd4b22891f7bf.fr_title: Beneficial Ownership Information Access and Safeguards, and Use of FinCEN Identifiers for Entities
88ecd4b22891f7bf.fr_dates_text: Written comments on this proposed rule may be submitted on or before February 14, 2023.

2cc39147e00eae94.plain_english: FinCEN is seeking comment on a routine Paperwork Reduction Act renewal, without change, of existing information collection requirements tied to the 2016 North Korea special measure. This is an administrative renewal with no substantive change to obligations.
2cc39147e00eae94.title: Agency Information Collection Activities; Proposed Renewal; Comment Request: Renewal Without Change of Information Collection Requirements in Connection With the Imposition of a Special Measure Concerning North Korea as a Jurisdiction of Primary Money Laundering Concern

bd4cff3fb42c9a42.plain_english: FinCEN proposed rules implementing the Corporate Transparency Act, requiring most entities to report beneficial owners and company applicants to FinCEN. This is a BSA/AML data source rather than a direct filing burden on banks, but it will eventually shape CDD and beneficial-ownership verification workflows once finalized.
bd4cff3fb42c9a42.title: Beneficial Ownership Information Reporting Requirements
bd4cff3fb42c9a42.fr_title: Beneficial Ownership Information Reporting Requirement Revision and Deadline Extension
bd4cff3fb42c9a42.fr_dates_text: This rule is effective March 26, 2025. Written comments must be received on or before May 27, 2025.

d65974cc57761e51.plain_english: FinCEN is seeking comment on whether to create a formal no-action letter process for BSA/AML matters, and how it would fit alongside existing administrative rulings and exemptive relief. This is an early-stage ANPRM affecting any institution subject to BSA obligations that might want prospective assurance from FinCEN; no rules are being set yet.
d65974cc57761e51.title: No-Action Letter Process

9ada391221e83d0c.plain_english: FinCEN is seeking comment on a routine PRA renewal of the CMIR (FinCEN Report 105) burden estimate, with no changes to the actual reporting requirement. This is an administrative paperwork notice with no substantive impact.
9ada391221e83d0c.title: Agency Information Collection Activities; Proposed Renewal; Comment Request; Renewal Without Change of the Report of International Transportation of Currency or Monetary Instruments; FinCEN Report 105

eff8085bce4f5799.plain_english: FinCEN issued an ANPRM exploring BSA reporting requirements for persons involved in real estate transactions to combat money laundering in the U.S. real estate sector. This targets real estate professionals and closing agents, not community banks or fintechs, and is only an early-stage request for comment.
eff8085bce4f5799.title: Anti-Money Laundering Regulations for Real Estate Transactions

02c02b5ac72aa6ae.plain_english: FinCEN proposed a limited pilot allowing institutions with SAR obligations to share SARs and related info with foreign branches, subsidiaries and affiliates to fight illicit finance, per the AML Act. Mainly relevant to institutions with foreign affiliates; most community banks lack such structures, though it touches BSA/AML sharing practices generally.
02c02b5ac72aa6ae.title: Pilot Program on Sharing of Suspicious Activity Reports and Related Information With Foreign Branches, Subsidiaries, and Affiliates
02c02b5ac72aa6ae.fr_title: Pilot Program on Sharing of Suspicious Activity Reports and Related Information With Foreign Branches, Subsidiaries, and Affiliates
02c02b5ac72aa6ae.fr_dates_text: Written comments on this proposed rule must be received on or before March 28, 2022.

4f9e68b6f54057b1.plain_english: FinCEN is removing obsolete FBAR civil penalty language that was superseded by the American Jobs Creation Act of 2004. This is a technical cleanup with no new obligations.
4f9e68b6f54057b1.title: Bank Secrecy Act Regulations-Reports of Foreign Financial Accounts Civil Penalties

d4ead4f121011aee.plain_english: FinCEN issued an RFI seeking public comment on how to streamline and modernize BSA/AML regulations and guidance, as part of its Section 6216 review under the AML Act. It's a solicitation for input, not a rule change, so no immediate obligations arise, but it signals potential future revisions to AML/CFT expectations affecting all BSA-covered institutions.
d4ead4f121011aee.title: Review of Bank Secrecy Act Regulations and Guidance

252bbff8819e1547.plain_english: NCUA proposes to streamline share-insurance disclosure rules for federally insured credit unions by removing redundant requirements around disclosing nonmember account coverage. Applies only to credit unions.
252bbff8819e1547.title: Requirements for Insurance
252bbff8819e1547.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received by March 30, 2026.

c50142c4e2938c3d.plain_english: NCUA proposes raising the major-assets prohibition thresholds under the Depository Institution Management Interlocks Act to $10 billion, updating figures unchanged since 1996, and removing a presumption tied to minority- or women-controlled institutions. This governance rule affects which people may serve as management officials at multiple credit unions.
c50142c4e2938c3d.title: Thresholds Increase for the Major Assets Prohibition of the Depository Institution Management Interlocks Act Rule
c50142c4e2938c3d.fr_title: Thresholds Increase for the Major Assets Prohibition of the Depository Institution Management Interlocks Act Rule
c50142c4e2938c3d.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received by July 6, 2026.

83b62d0bb11e5536.plain_english: NCUA is seeking comment on streamlining its credit union data collections (Call Report, Corporate Call Report, and Profile forms). Credit unions may see changes to how and what they report, though this is early-stage information gathering with no immediate obligations.
83b62d0bb11e5536.title: Request for Information Regarding Enhancing and Streamlining Data Collection From Credit Unions
83b62d0bb11e5536.fr_title: Request for Information Regarding Enhancing and Streamlining Data Collection From Credit Unions
83b62d0bb11e5536.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received by June 23, 2026.

7ab6deec3bcca214.plain_english: NCUA proposes to relax procedural, disclosure, and member-communication requirements for credit unions converting/merging into banks, giving CU boards more flexibility. This affects credit unions considering conversion to a bank charter and the members whose voting/disclosure protections would change.
7ab6deec3bcca214.title: Bank Conversions and Mergers, Subpart C-Merger of Insured Credit Unions Into Banks
7ab6deec3bcca214.fr_title: Bank Conversions and Mergers, Subpart C-Merger of Insured Credit Unions Into Banks
7ab6deec3bcca214.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received on or before June 22, 2026.

b55a2725f855dd0e.plain_english: NCUA is renewing existing information collections through OMB under the Paperwork Reduction Act. This is a routine administrative PRA notice with no substantive rule change.
b55a2725f855dd0e.title: Proposed New and Renewal of Agency Information Collections; Request for Comments

667ad2f2aa293d0c.plain_english: NCUA proposes to loosen associational common-bond rules so that requiring a product or service purchase no longer automatically disqualifies a group from field-of-membership eligibility. This expands chartering flexibility for federal credit unions seeking to add associational groups.
667ad2f2aa293d0c.title: Chartering and Field of Membership
667ad2f2aa293d0c.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received on or before June 8, 2026.

8e9f9a39e10cd588.plain_english: NCUA proposes replacing prescriptive requirements for third-party servicing of indirect vehicle loans with a principles-based approach, giving credit unions more flexibility in managing these servicing arrangements. This affects federally-insured credit unions engaged in indirect auto lending. The summary itself notes it as a credit-union matter; credit unions are in scope on the same footing as community banks.
8e9f9a39e10cd588.title: Third-Party Servicing of Indirect Vehicle Loans
8e9f9a39e10cd588.fr_title: Third-Party Servicing of Indirect Vehicle Loans
8e9f9a39e10cd588.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received by May 26, 2026.

e30ffcf3dabac6cb.plain_english: NCUA is asking for comment under its voluntary EGRPRA review to identify outdated or burdensome credit-union rules across Agency Programs, Capital, and Consumer Protection categories. This affects federally-insured credit unions only; it's a housekeeping review with no immediate rule changes.
e30ffcf3dabac6cb.title: Regulatory Publication and Voluntary Review as Contemplated by the Economic Growth and Regulatory Paperwork Reduction Act of 1996
e30ffcf3dabac6cb.fr_title: Regulatory Publication and Voluntary Review as Contemplated by the Economic Growth and Regulatory Paperwork Reduction Act of 1996
e30ffcf3dabac6cb.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received by June 1, 2026.

3f5b313e4d948b42.plain_english: NCUA is routinely renewing previously approved information collections with OMB under the Paperwork Reduction Act. This is an administrative, credit-union-focused notice with no substantive regulatory change.
3f5b313e4d948b42.title: Renewal of Agency Information Collections of Previously Approved Collections; Request for Comments

9f01d7f4463c530f.plain_english: NCUA proposes to delete a redundant definition from its statutory lien rules for federal credit unions. It's a technical cleanup with no substantive effect on how liens operate.
9f01d7f4463c530f.title: Statutory Liens
9f01d7f4463c530f.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received on or before April 27, 2026.

9949d93295ffcbff.plain_english: NCUA proposes to eliminate a provision governing federal credit union service contracts to reduce administrative burden. Affects federal credit unions' contracting obligations; a deregulatory operational change with limited immediate impact.
9949d93295ffcbff.title: Credit Union Service Contracts
9949d93295ffcbff.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received by April 27, 2026.

1bbbc2455984e643.plain_english: NCUA proposes to rescind a redundant regulation on refunding interest to credit union members because the FCU Act already provides the authority. This is a credit-union-only administrative cleanup with no substantive change to obligations.
1bbbc2455984e643.title: Refund of Interest
1bbbc2455984e643.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received by April 27, 2026

a046ce15ad252751.plain_english: NCUA proposes to replace prescriptive policy-content lists and conflict-of-interest/compensation requirements governing how federal credit unions purchase, sell, and pledge eligible obligations with a principles-based approach. This is an administrative loosening affecting federal credit unions' loan-portfolio transaction practices.
a046ce15ad252751.title: Purchase, Sale, and Pledge of Eligible Obligations
a046ce15ad252751.fr_title: Purchase, Sale, and Pledge of Eligible Obligations
a046ce15ad252751.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received by April 27, 2026.

83b25d0419848ba9.plain_english: NCUA proposes to relax restrictions on compensating credit union officials and employees for member loans, permitting lending metrics in incentive and bonus plans tied to overall financial performance. Applies to federally-insured credit unions; no impact on banks or fintechs.
83b25d0419848ba9.title: Compensation in Connection With Loans to Members and Lines of Credit to Members
83b25d0419848ba9.fr_title: Compensation in Connection With Loans to Members and Lines of Credit to Members
83b25d0419848ba9.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received by April 27, 2026.

25d8d6910853d0fb.plain_english: NCUA proposes to eliminate the requirement that federal credit union directors become familiar with finance and accounting within six months of joining the board. This affects credit union governance and director obligations, easing onboarding expectations for new board members.
25d8d6910853d0fb.title: Post-Election Training for New Board Members
25d8d6910853d0fb.fr_title: Post-Election Training for New Board Members
25d8d6910853d0fb.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received by April 27, 2026.

fcb83496d5124e29.plain_english: The NCUA proposed rules implementing the GENIUS Act's stablecoin regime, setting up a licensing and supervision process for payment stablecoin issuers that are subsidiaries of federally insured credit unions, and limiting FICUs to investing only in NCUA-licensed PPSIs. This is credit-union-focused, but signals the emerging federal stablecoin licensing framework under the GENIUS Act. NCUA must finalize implementing regs by July 18, 2026.
fcb83496d5124e29.title: Investments in and Licensing of Permitted Payment Stablecoins Issuers
fcb83496d5124e29.fr_title: Investments in and Licensing of Permitted Payment Stablecoins Issuers
fcb83496d5124e29.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received by April 13, 2026.

b561a160985326fe.plain_english: NCUA proposes to rescind IRPS 08-2, cutting the number of sources federal credit unions must consult for chartering and field-of-membership compliance. This is a credit-union-only administrative streamlining matter with no fintech dimension.
b561a160985326fe.title: Chartering and Field of Membership for Federal Credit Unions-Interpretive Rulings and Policy Statements
b561a160985326fe.fr_title: Chartering and Field of Membership for Federal Credit Unions-Interpretive Rulings and Policy Statements
b561a160985326fe.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received on or before April 13, 2026.

aec77dba1aa83113.plain_english: NCUA proposes to relax formatting requirements for member disclosure statements when a credit union voluntarily terminates or converts its federal share insurance. This is a narrow administrative change affecting the disclosure process for credit unions pursuing insurance termination.
aec77dba1aa83113.title: Mergers of Insured Credit Unions Into Other Credit Unions; Voluntary Termination or Conversion of Insured Status
aec77dba1aa83113.fr_title: Mergers of Insured Credit Unions Into Other Credit Unions; Voluntary Termination or Conversion of Insured Status
aec77dba1aa83113.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received on or before April 13, 2026.

34aeb72daf677598.plain_english: NCUA proposes to streamline the rules governing credit union conversions to mutual savings banks by dropping prescriptive procedural and disclosure requirements. This affects federally-insured credit unions contemplating a charter conversion, easing the process mechanics.
34aeb72daf677598.title: Bank Conversions and Mergers, Subpart A-Conversion of Insured Credit Unions to Mutual Savings Banks
34aeb72daf677598.fr_title: Bank Conversions and Mergers, Subpart A-Conversion of Insured Credit Unions to Mutual Savings Banks
34aeb72daf677598.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received on or before April 13, 2026.

585e0749f0ab42f9.plain_english: NCUA is submitting a routine Paperwork Reduction Act information collection to OMB for comment. It carries no substantive regulatory change and is purely administrative.
585e0749f0ab42f9.title: Proposed Collection; Request for Comments

97354e257298bea5.plain_english: NCUA proposes adjusting the timing of member-notice requirements when a credit union terminates excess non-federal share insurance coverage. This affects credit unions that carry private excess insurance above the federal share-insurance limit; it is a narrow administrative notice-timing change.
97354e257298bea5.title: Notice of Termination of Excess Insurance Coverage
97354e257298bea5.fr_title: Notice of Termination of Excess Insurance Coverage
97354e257298bea5.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received by March 30, 2026.

1feac895f2051e1c.plain_english: NCUA proposes removing its regulatory maximum borrowing authority provision for federally insured credit unions because it duplicates the statutory limit for federal credit unions. This is administrative deregulation affecting credit unions only, with no substantive change to borrowing capacity.
1feac895f2051e1c.title: Requirements for Insurance; Maximum Borrowing Authority
1feac895f2051e1c.fr_title: Requirements for Insurance; Maximum Borrowing Authority
1feac895f2051e1c.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received by March 30, 2026.

92e56af346526d99.plain_english: NCUA proposes to eliminate the written-plan documentation requirement for credit unions whose borrowings, public unit, and nonmember shares together exceed 70% of capital, moving to a principles-based supervisory approach. This affects credit union funding and liquidity management practices.
92e56af346526d99.title: Public Unit and Nonmember Shares
92e56af346526d99.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received by March 30, 2026.

16ae64bc7da6a81e.plain_english: Routine NCUA Paperwork Reduction Act notice submitting an information collection to OMB for review. No substantive regulatory change.
16ae64bc7da6a81e.title: Agency Information Collection for Comments Request: Proposed Collection

8885a1b2ab41e7c5.plain_english: NCUA proposes to relax governance and filing requirements for corporate credit unions, dropping the ALCO board-member mandate and certain accountant-report filings. This affects corporate credit unions specifically, a narrow segment; retail/community credit unions are only indirectly touched.
8885a1b2ab41e7c5.title: Corporate Credit Unions
8885a1b2ab41e7c5.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received by March 16, 2026.

8ef29061f961136e.plain_english: NCUA proposes deleting an outdated, redundant regulation that summarized Fair Housing Act nondiscrimination rules for federal credit unions. This is a housekeeping change with no substantive impact on credit union operations.
8ef29061f961136e.title: Nondiscrimination Requirements
8ef29061f961136e.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received on or before March 16, 2026.

5dc5bb773786bcdb.plain_english: NCUA issued an interim final rule correcting the numbering of a system of records and exempting one from certain Privacy Act requirements. This is an internal administrative/recordkeeping matter specific to NCUA and does not affect community banks or fintechs.
5dc5bb773786bcdb.title: Privacy Act Exemption; Correction

b8e5befc7512f8a8.plain_english: The NCUA proposes to eliminate segregated deposit and collateral requirements for federally insured credit unions that act as surety or guarantor, giving them more flexibility in structuring such products. Credit unions are in scope on the same footing as community banks, so this affects FICU product design.
b8e5befc7512f8a8.title: Suretyship and Guaranty; Segregated Deposit and Collateral
b8e5befc7512f8a8.fr_title: Suretyship and Guaranty; Segregated Deposit and Collateral
b8e5befc7512f8a8.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received by February 27, 2026.

db5f974651d1e5c3.plain_english: NCUA proposes to give federally insured credit unions more time to report catastrophic acts and to eliminate the specific documentation checklist. This is an administrative burden reduction affecting credit-union reporting obligations only.
db5f974651d1e5c3.title: Catastrophic Act Reporting
db5f974651d1e5c3.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received on or before February 27, 2026.

c52d5ce2b1294287.plain_english: NCUA proposes to strip out its regulatory provisions governing approval and policies for credit unions lending to other credit unions, leaving only the underlying statutory requirements. This affects federally-insured credit unions engaged in inter-CU lending; the practical impact is reduced regulatory prescription.
c52d5ce2b1294287.title: Limits on Loans to Other Credit Unions
c52d5ce2b1294287.fr_title: Limits on Loans to Other Credit Unions
c52d5ce2b1294287.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received by February 27, 2026.

5903570005a7dd23.plain_english: NCUA proposes to eliminate the official advertising statement requirement, streamlining advertising rules for federally insured credit unions. This is a credit-union-only change affecting how CUs disclose insured status in ads.
5903570005a7dd23.title: Accuracy of Advertising and Notice of Insured Status
5903570005a7dd23.fr_title: Accuracy of Advertising and Notice of Insured Status
5903570005a7dd23.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received by February 27, 2026.

b027aec2df7c225c.plain_english: NCUA proposes to relocate its data-breach response and member-notification guidance out of the CFR (Appendix B to part 748) and reissue it as standalone guidance for federally insured credit unions. It's an administrative streamlining that doesn't change substantive breach-response or notice obligations.
b027aec2df7c225c.title: Guidance on Response Programs for Unauthorized Access to Member Information and Member Notice
b027aec2df7c225c.fr_title: Guidance on Response Programs for Unauthorized Access to Member Information and Member Notice
b027aec2df7c225c.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received on or before February 9, 2026.

4bb4cf9da17f9881.plain_english: The NCUA proposes to relocate its member-information safeguarding guidelines (Appendix A to part 748) out of the CFR and republish them as a Letter to Credit Unions, with no substantive change. This is an administrative housekeeping move affecting federally-insured credit unions only.
4bb4cf9da17f9881.title: Guidelines for Safeguarding Member Information
4bb4cf9da17f9881.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received on or before February 9, 2026.

9798618b9ce0998f.plain_english: NCUA proposes to streamline its supervisory committee audit and verification requirements for credit unions, removing outdated or duplicative provisions. This affects federally-insured credit unions' internal audit obligations; the summary characterizes it as credit-union-only, but credit unions are in our scope on the same footing as community banks.
9798618b9ce0998f.title: Supervisory Committee Audits and Verifications
9798618b9ce0998f.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received by February 9, 2026.

97bafde3d54174d4.plain_english: NCUA is renewing previously approved information collections under the Paperwork Reduction Act and submitting them to OMB. This is a routine administrative PRA notice with no substantive regulatory change.
97bafde3d54174d4.title: Renewal of Agency Information Collection of a Previously Approved Collection; Request for Comments

d9f7daf036e9e752.plain_english: The NCUA released its staff draft 2026-2027 budget for public comment. It is an administrative funding notice with no substantive regulatory impact on credit unions or fintechs.
d9f7daf036e9e752.title: The NCUA Staff Draft 2026-2027 Budget

a06e22ada234e2de.plain_english: NCUA proposes to eliminate reputation risk as a supervisory category and prohibit examiners from pressuring credit unions to close accounts or deny services based on political/religious views or lawful-but-disfavored activities. Hits federally-insured credit unions, which are in our scope alongside community banks. Reduces examiner discretion around 'debanking' concerns.
a06e22ada234e2de.title: Prohibition on Use of Reputation Risk by NCUA
a06e22ada234e2de.fr_title: Prohibition on Use of Reputation Risk by NCUA
a06e22ada234e2de.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received by December 22, 2025.

5f7c5feb1113dfab.plain_english: NCUA opened its 2025 Community Development Revolving Loan Fund grant round, offering technical assistance grants to low-income-designated credit unions. This is a voluntary funding opportunity for qualifying CUs, not a rule change or compliance obligation.
5f7c5feb1113dfab.title: 2025 Community Development Revolving Loan Fund
5f7c5feb1113dfab.fr_dates_text: This NOFO is effective on the date of publication in the Federal Register. NCUA will accept applications beginning on October 15, 2025, at 12 p.m. Eastern Time. Applications must be submitted by December 16, 2025 at 11:59 p.m. Eastern Time. Applications submitted after the application closing date and time will not be considered. Applicants will be notified of NCUA's decision after the agency's review is completed.

e8ceefa0e51676a2.plain_english: NCUA is holding a public hearing on one credit union's request to expand its community charter to the Miami area (population 2.5M+). This is a single-institution field-of-membership matter with no broader rule change; it affects only the applicant credit union.
e8ceefa0e51676a2.title: Chartering and Field of Membership-Public Hearing; Community Charter Application for Area With a Population of 2.5 Million or More
e8ceefa0e51676a2.fr_title: Chartering and Field of Membership-Public Hearing; Community Charter Application for Area With a Population of 2.5 Million or More
e8ceefa0e51676a2.fr_dates_text: The public hearing will be held on September 23, 2025, at 1 p.m. Eastern. Interested parties seeking to make presentations during the hearing must register by August 26, 2025, to be placed on a list of presenters. The applicant along with no more than seven other interested parties may request to make presentations. The first six interested parties that contact the NCUA in writing will be permitted to make such presentations. Members of the public seeking to watch or listen to the virtual meeting must register to view the public hearing. The agency will not reimburse any travel expenses for interested parties making presentations, or members of the public attending the meeting. Written comments must be submitted by August 26, 2025.

79a11448a34e8087.plain_english: NCUA issued a notice describing how it will approach criminally liable regulatory offenses in response to an executive order on overcriminalization. It's a credit-union-focused administrative/policy statement about enforcement referral practices, with no substantive change to compliance obligations.
79a11448a34e8087.title: Guidance on Referrals for Potential Criminal Enforcement

04c631638415397e.plain_english: NCUA proposes to extend the Call Report (Form 5300) collection with revised instructions for credit unions. This is a routine paperwork-renewal notice affecting how credit unions file quarterly financial data; comment is invited but there is no substantive rule change.
04c631638415397e.effective_or_comment_date: Revised instructions proposed to take effect for the September 30, 2025 report date
04c631638415397e.title: Proposed Agency Information Collection Activities; Comment Request
04c631638415397e.fr_title: Proposed Agency Information Collection Activities; Comment Request
04c631638415397e.fr_dates_text: Interested persons are invited to submit comments on or before June 22, 2026.

a85f00f0295839c0.plain_english: NCUA reopened the comment period on two finalized credit-union rules — share insurance simplification and succession planning — as part of the January 2025 regulatory freeze. Credit unions may want to weigh in, but this is an administrative reopening rather than a new substantive change.
a85f00f0295839c0.title: Simplification of Share Insurance and Succession Planning Final Rules; Solicitation of Comments
a85f00f0295839c0.fr_title: Simplification of Share Insurance and Succession Planning Final Rules; Solicitation of Comments
a85f00f0295839c0.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received on or before June 23, 2025.

31665271cef36990.plain_english: The NCUA cancelled a scheduled Sunshine Act meeting. This is a routine administrative notice with no substantive regulatory content.
31665271cef36990.title: Sunshine Act Meetings; Cancellation

647b9ffea484654d.plain_english: NCUA and the agencies granted temporary exceptions to FIRREA appraisal requirements for real estate transactions in Los Angeles County following the wildfire disaster declaration, effective until January 8, 2028. This is localized disaster-relief appraisal accommodation, not relevant to our BaaS/fintech or community bank credit-risk focus.
647b9ffea484654d.title: Temporary Exceptions to FIRREA Appraisal Requirements in Los Angeles County as Affected by California Wildfires and Straight-Line Winds

926dc053a0f481e1.plain_english: NCUA made its routine annual inflation adjustment to the maximum civil monetary penalties it can assess. This affects federally-insured credit unions under NCUA authority; it's a statutory housekeeping update with no substantive change to conduct requirements.
926dc053a0f481e1.title: Civil Monetary Penalty Inflation Adjustment

82bc09eee653b7c7.plain_english: Routine NCUA Paperwork Reduction Act notice submitting previously approved information collections to OMB for renewal. Purely administrative and credit-union-specific with no substantive regulatory change.
82bc09eee653b7c7.title: Revision of Agency Information Collection of a Previously Approved Collection; Request for Comments

e2de4c00cf997f30.plain_english: NCUA finalized a rule requiring all federally insured credit unions to adopt and periodically review written succession plans for key positions like directors and senior management. This is a governance mandate hitting every FICU, aimed at ensuring continuity and reducing failure/merger risk from leadership gaps.
e2de4c00cf997f30.title: Succession Planning
e2de4c00cf997f30.fr_dates_text: This final rule is effective on January 1, 2026.

3054b87752c9bb0d.plain_english: FinCEN issued an advisory (FIN-2025-A003) on the use of Chinese money laundering networks by Mexico-based transnational criminal organizations to launder illicit proceeds. It flags typologies and red flags institutions should watch for in BSA/AML monitoring and SAR filings. Affects any institution with related exposure, requiring updated transaction monitoring and reporting.
3054b87752c9bb0d.title: Aviso de FinCEN FIN-2025-A003

3a1f30848e7c6200.plain_english: FinCEN issued an alert flagging fraud rings exploiting federal child nutrition programs in Minnesota, giving red flags and typologies to help institutions detect and report related suspicious activity. This is a BSA/AML detection and SAR-filing matter affecting any institution processing payments tied to these schemes, not a fintech-specific rule change.
3a1f30848e7c6200.title: FIN-2026-Alert001

3029a75f9ff53fc1.plain_english: FinCEN issued an alert directing financial institutions to watch for and report cross-border funds transfers tied to illegal immigration. Banks and money transmitters should review transaction monitoring and SAR filing practices for cross-border remittance activity flagged in the alert. This is a BSA/AML monitoring and reporting matter affecting institutions handling cross-border payments.
3029a75f9ff53fc1.title: FIN-2025-Alert003

a28e58d3416f79e5.plain_english: FinCEN issued a notice on financially motivated sextortion, alerting financial institutions to detect and report related suspicious activity. It provides typologies and red flags to support SAR filing on this exploitation scheme. Relevant to BSA/AML programs across banks and money service businesses.
a28e58d3416f79e5.title: FIN-2025-NTC2

cf85d910153a5f78.plain_english: FinCEN issued an advisory warning of Chinese money laundering networks helping Mexico-based TCOs launder drug proceeds, providing red flags and typologies for detection. Institutions with BSA/AML programs should incorporate these indicators into monitoring and consider related SAR filings. This is a general BSA/AML advisory relevant to any bank or MSB, not fintech-specific.
cf85d910153a5f78.title: FinCEN Advisory FIN-2025-A003

eb66b60dfd03c0b8.plain_english: FinCEN issued a notice flagging the misuse of convertible virtual currency (CVC) kiosks/crypto ATMs for scam payments and other illicit activity, providing red flags and reminding covered institutions of SAR filing expectations. This hits MSBs operating crypto kiosks and banks with crypto/CVC exposure, who should tune monitoring and reporting to the described typologies.
eb66b60dfd03c0b8.title: FIN-2025-NTC1

2ecf6318916840e5.plain_english: FinCEN published a retrospective summary of its FY2024 activities across BSA/AML enforcement, rulemaking, and priorities. It's informational with no new obligations, though it may signal FinCEN's focus areas for institutions with AML programs.
2ecf6318916840e5.title: FinCEN Year in Review for FY24

a6a494c546f2ba05.plain_english: FinCEN issued an advisory alerting financial institutions to red flags associated with Iran's oil smuggling, shadow banking networks, and weapons procurement. BSA/AML teams should update monitoring and SAR filing to detect these typologies. Applies broadly to any institution with BSA obligations, including community banks and MSBs.
a6a494c546f2ba05.title: FinCEN Advisory FIN-2025-A002

3344a13ebd1fefb4.plain_english: FinCEN issued an alert warning financial institutions about oil smuggling schemes tied to Mexico-based cartels along the Southwest border, providing red flags and reminding institutions of SAR reporting expectations. This is a BSA/AML typology alert relevant to any institution's transaction monitoring and suspicious-activity detection, particularly those with exposure to cross-border energy trade.
3344a13ebd1fefb4.title: FIN-2025-Alert002

bcd9f80f1f191812.plain_english: FinCEN issued an alert warning financial institutions about bulk cash smuggling and repatriation schemes used by Mexico-based transnational criminal organizations. It hits BSA/AML compliance teams, which should review typologies and red flags and factor them into SAR filing and monitoring. This is general BSA/AML guidance rather than a fintech-specific matter.
bcd9f80f1f191812.title: FIN-2025-Alert001

67ab26803095b0d8.plain_english: FinCEN extended the Corporate Transparency Act beneficial ownership information (BOI) reporting deadline by 30 days and signaled it will revise the reporting rule. This affects reporting companies subject to BOI filing obligations, giving them additional time while the rule's scope is reconsidered.
67ab26803095b0d8.title: FinCEN Notice FIN-2025-CTA1

eb7a9ba942615665.plain_english: FinCEN warned about fraud schemes that impersonate FinCEN using its name, seal, and authority to trick victims into sending money or sharing information. This is an informational fraud alert; institutions may want to flag it to customers and fraud teams but it imposes no new obligations.
eb7a9ba942615665.title: FIN-2024-Alert005

6cde2f1c2bee1adc.plain_english: FinCEN issued an alert warning financial institutions about fraud schemes using deepfake media and generative AI to circumvent identity verification and open fraudulent accounts. It flags red flags for detecting synthetic or manipulated identity documents and images, and reminds institutions of related SAR reporting expectations. Any institution relying on remote/digital identity verification—especially those with heavy online onboarding—should tighten controls.
6cde2f1c2bee1adc.title: FIN-2024-Alert004

c5916cf7482d9b9a.plain_english: FinCEN issued an alert to help financial institutions identify and report transactions potentially tied to financing of Hizballah's terrorist activities. Institutions should apply the red flags in their BSA/AML monitoring and SAR filing processes. This is a general BSA/AML terror-finance advisory affecting all covered institutions.
c5916cf7482d9b9a.title: FIN-2024-Alert003

3d31baf16a884804.plain_english: FinCEN finalized a rule imposing AML/CFT program and SAR requirements on certain SEC-registered investment advisers and exempt reporting advisers. This targets the investment adviser sector, not community banks, BaaS arrangements, or the fintech verticals we track.
3d31baf16a884804.title: FinCEN Investment Adviser Final Rule Fact Sheet

8abdbbb30e5231d8.plain_english: FinCEN finalized a rule requiring reporting of certain non-financed residential real estate transfers to legal entities and trusts, aimed at combating money laundering through real estate. This targets settlement agents, title companies and other real estate professionals, not community banks or fintechs in our focus areas.
8abdbbb30e5231d8.title: FinCEN Residential Real Estate Final Rule Fact Sheet

e0e68783d6e57d1e.plain_english: FinCEN, OFAC, and the FBI issued a joint notice alerting financial institutions to timeshare fraud schemes tied to Mexico-based transnational criminal organizations, providing red flags and reminding institutions of SAR filing expectations. This is a BSA/AML typology alert relevant to any institution's suspicious-activity monitoring, not a rule change.
e0e68783d6e57d1e.title: FinCEN Notice FIN-2024-NTC2

3f3ba5887f0e4962.plain_english: FinCEN's supplemental alert on Israeli extremist violence in the West Bank has been rescinded as of January 29, 2025. This was a geopolitical illicit-finance alert unrelated to community bank or fintech operations.
3f3ba5887f0e4962.title: FIN-2024-Alert002

2a6a56a829b13e9a.plain_english: FinCEN published a retrospective summary of its FY 2023 activities, including SAR/CTR statistics and BSA/AML priorities. It's informational context rather than a new requirement, useful for benchmarking but imposing no new obligations.
2a6a56a829b13e9a.title: FinCEN Year in Review for FY 2023

41b03158ba3c0e04.plain_english: FinCEN alerts financial institutions to fraud schemes using counterfeit U.S. passport cards for identity theft, and provides red flags and SAR filing guidance. This affects onboarding and identity-verification controls across all institutions, particularly those relying on passport cards as ID.
41b03158ba3c0e04.title: FinCEN Notice FIN-2024-NTC1

988b955790e2aa42.plain_english: FinCEN's alert on Israeli extremist settler violence against Palestinians in the West Bank has been rescinded as of January 29, 2025. It was a narrow geopolitical SAR-filing advisory with no bearing on community bank or fintech operations, and it is no longer in effect.
988b955790e2aa42.title: FIN-2024-Alert001

ffb1f848f8cfeb49.plain_english: FinCEN issued an alert warning financial institutions about fraud involving the COVID-19 Employee Retention Credit, providing red flags and reminding institutions of SAR filing expectations. This is a BSA/AML monitoring matter affecting all covered institutions, not a fintech-specific rule.
ffb1f848f8cfeb49.title: FIN-2023-Alert007

998aa771d893c3cc.plain_english: FinCEN and BIS introduced a new SAR key term ('FIN-2023-GLOBALEXPORT') and outlined red flags to help institutions detect and report transactions tied to evasion of U.S. export controls, particularly involving Russia. Banks and MSBs should incorporate these red flags into their BSA/AML monitoring and use the key term when filing related SARs.
998aa771d893c3cc.title: FinCEN Notice FIN-2023-NTC2

8d94c81f39414f4a.plain_english: FinCEN issued an alert urging financial institutions to identify and report potential financing activity tied to Hamas following the October 2023 attacks. Institutions should apply heightened vigilance to transactions with terrorism-financing red flags and reference the alert's key terms in SAR filings. This is a BSA/AML compliance expectation applicable across banks and MSBs.
8d94c81f39414f4a.title: FIN-2023-Alert006

019fe6c0296d1c45.plain_english: FinCEN issued an alert warning financial institutions about 'pig butchering' virtual currency investment scams, which combine romance/confidence fraud with fake crypto investment platforms. It provides red flags and SAR filing guidance, hitting institutions handling crypto-related transactions and any bank whose customers may be victims wiring funds to scammers.
019fe6c0296d1c45.title: FIN-2023-Alert005

1ac1aa7c772b1816.plain_english: FinCEN published a financial trend analysis of ransomware activity reported in BSA data for the first half of 2021, showing a sharp rise in ransomware-related SARs and payments, often involving convertible virtual currency. It informs BSA/AML programs on detecting and reporting ransomware-related transactions but imposes no new requirements.
1ac1aa7c772b1816.title: Financial Trend Analysis

7a2f81b70d9bef87.plain_english: FinCEN issued a notice alerting financial institutions to payroll tax evasion and workers' compensation fraud schemes in the construction industry, providing red flags and a reporting key term for SAR filings. This is a BSA/AML typology alert relevant to any institution's SAR monitoring, particularly those serving construction-sector clients.
7a2f81b70d9bef87.title: FinCEN Notice FIN-2023-NTC1

5538c11b829399ce.plain_english: FinCEN and BIS issued a supplemental alert urging financial institutions to stay vigilant for attempts to evade Russian export controls, providing updated red flags and reporting expectations. This affects BSA/AML monitoring and SAR filing practices at all institutions, requiring updated transaction screening for suspected export-control evasion.
5538c11b829399ce.title: FIN-2023-Alert004

b7e0e8ed690bf4af.plain_english: FinCEN published a retrospective summarizing its FY2022 activities, including SAR/CTR statistics and priorities. It's informational with no new requirements, useful for BSA/AML context but not actionable.
b7e0e8ed690bf4af.title: FinCEN Year in Review for FY 2022

9badcf4e933e46e9.plain_english: FinCEN issued an alert warning of a nationwide surge in check fraud tied to mail theft, providing red flags and SAR filing instructions. Banks should update BSA/AML monitoring and SAR procedures to flag mail-theft-related check fraud and use the specified key term when filing.
9badcf4e933e46e9.title: FIN-2023-Alert003
9badcf4e933e46e9.url: https://www.fincen.gov/system/files/shared/FinCEN Alert Mail Theft-Related Check Fraud FINAL 508.pdf

2c63ffd5eecf78d3.plain_english: FinCEN issued an alert warning financial institutions to watch for sanctioned Russian elites and their proxies using US commercial real estate to evade sanctions and launder funds. Banks should apply enhanced due diligence and file SARs on suspicious CRE-related transactions involving these actors. This is a BSA/AML compliance flag rather than a rule change.
2c63ffd5eecf78d3.title: FIN-2023-Alert002

5411383e535d6ed4.plain_english: FinCEN issued an alert warning financial institutions to be vigilant for transactions tied to human smuggling along the US Southwest border and to file SARs with the associated key term. This is a BSA/AML red-flag advisory affecting all institutions' suspicious activity monitoring, not a change in rules.
5411383e535d6ed4.title: FIN-2023-Alert001

35fe53bc7c13d9ef.plain_english: FinCEN and BIS issued a joint alert urging financial institutions to watch for attempts to evade export controls imposed on Russia and Belarus, providing red flags and reporting expectations. This applies broadly to banks and money services businesses, adding a sanctions/export-evasion dimension to existing BSA/AML monitoring.
35fe53bc7c13d9ef.title: FIN-2022-Alert003

f7d4d4dccc9dee4b.plain_english: FinCEN alerted financial institutions to watch for attempts by sanctioned Russian elites and oligarchs to hide or launder wealth through real estate, luxury goods, and other high-value assets. Institutions should apply enhanced scrutiny and file SARs on suspicious activity tied to these parties. This is a BSA/AML sanctions-evasion typology alert applicable to all covered institutions.
f7d4d4dccc9dee4b.title: FIN-2022-Alert002

2979cc935ecae60b.plain_english: FinCEN issued an alert urging financial institutions to increase vigilance for Russian sanctions evasion following the invasion of Ukraine, flagging red flags including use of convertible virtual currency to bypass OFAC restrictions. It calls out CVC exchangers, MSBs and institutions handling crypto to detect and report suspicious activity tied to sanctioned parties. Fintechs and crypto-facing firms in particular should tighten transaction monitoring and SAR reporting.
2979cc935ecae60b.title: FIN-2022-Alert001

6e5b949348bdb907.plain_english: FinCEN published a fact sheet describing its Rapid Response Program (RRP), which helps victims of cyber-enabled financial crime and fraud recover funds transferred to fraudulent accounts through coordination with law enforcement and foreign authorities. It's informational and outlines how institutions and victims can engage the program, not a new obligation.
6e5b949348bdb907.title: FinCEN's RRP Fact Sheet- FIN-2022-FCT1

1d05cfa8c0ca4f8e.plain_english: FinCEN issued a financial threat analysis on illicit finance tied to wildlife trafficking, highlighting red flags and typologies for BSA reporting. This is BSA/AML awareness material relevant to institutions' SAR monitoring, but it is general in nature and not fintech-specific.
1d05cfa8c0ca4f8e.title: Financial Threat Analysis

f7acf333d926dcb0.plain_english: FinCEN issued a notice urging financial institutions to be alert to environmental crimes (illegal logging, fishing, wildlife trafficking, waste/hazardous substances trafficking) and related money laundering, and to reference the notice in SAR filings. This is a BSA/AML awareness item affecting all covered institutions' suspicious activity monitoring and reporting.
f7acf333d926dcb0.title: FinCEN Notice FIN-2021-NTC4

6e18ee57c631844b.plain_english: FinCEN issued a notice highlighting online child sexual exploitation crimes and reminding financial institutions of SAR filing considerations and relevant red flags. This is a BSA/AML awareness notice affecting all financial institutions' suspicious activity monitoring, not a change in rules.
6e18ee57c631844b.title: FinCEN Notice FIN-2021-NTC3

e155a01d33580d33.plain_english: FinCEN alerted financial institutions to money laundering and illicit finance risks tied to trade in antiquities and art, and previewed forthcoming rulemaking on antiquities dealers under the AML Act of 2020. Institutions with exposure to such transactions should watch for suspicious activity and file SARs where appropriate.
e155a01d33580d33.title: FinCEN Notice FIN-2021-NTC2

3736bf6c6b1b7fd7.plain_english: FinCEN consolidated its COVID-19-related SAR filing instructions and key terms into a single reference. This affects all BSA-obligated institutions filing SARs on pandemic-related fraud, providing standardized terminology for flagging such reports.
3736bf6c6b1b7fd7.title: FinCEN Notice FIN-2021-NTC1

7c5f4608a63b9ac4.plain_english: FinCEN alerts financial institutions to COVID-19 vaccine-related fraud, scams, and cyberattacks, urging vigilance and SAR filing where appropriate. This is general BSA/AML fraud-awareness guidance applicable to all financial institutions, not a fintech-specific matter.
7c5f4608a63b9ac4.title: FinCEN Notice FIN-2020-NTC4

b14574775698a4c6.plain_english: FinCEN issued an alert flagging a convertible virtual currency scam tied to a high-profile Twitter account compromise, warning institutions to watch for related fraudulent CVC transactions. Hits crypto exchanges, MSBs and banks handling virtual currency flows, and informs SAR filing and BSA/AML monitoring for scam-related transfers.
b14574775698a4c6.title: FIN-2020-Alert001

bb41284b4fae80c3.plain_english: FinCEN issued a companion notice pointing to its COVID-19 advisories, which flag emerging fraud and illicit finance typologies during the pandemic. It signals BSA/AML monitoring and SAR reporting expectations around COVID-related scams. General BSA/AML guidance applicable to all filers, not specific to fintech arrangements.
bb41284b4fae80c3.title: FinCEN Notice FIN-2020-NTC3

1010f62047fd30a7.plain_english: FinCEN issued a COVID-19 notice reminding financial institutions of BSA/AML obligations, pandemic-related fraud typologies, and channels for reporting operational disruptions. It's a general BSA/AML notice applicable to all covered institutions, not a rule change.
1010f62047fd30a7.title: FinCEN Notice FIN-2020-NTC2

7cccc4720240add8.plain_english: FinCEN issued a notice reminding financial institutions to remain alert to COVID-19-related fraud and illicit activity while acknowledging potential BSA reporting delays during the pandemic. It applies broadly to all BSA-obligated institutions and signals FinCEN's expectations around continued vigilance and communication during disruptions.
7cccc4720240add8.title: FinCEN Notice FIN-2020-NTC1

aa7ed323ebc5c21b.plain_english: FinCEN reminds filers that Currency Transaction Reports submitted via XML batch filing will no longer be accepted in ASCII format as of June 1, 2018. This is a technical filing-format change affecting BSA reporting for institutions that batch-file CTRs.
aa7ed323ebc5c21b.title: CTR XML Batch Filing Deadline

38f0f5303fb5d8be.plain_english: NCUA finalized a rule on vital records preservation for credit unions, a routine administrative/operational requirement. It hits federally-insured credit unions but carries no substantive strategic or fintech implications.
38f0f5303fb5d8be.title: NCUA Board Approves Final Rule on Vital Records Preservation

5a4edc2e04679438.plain_english: NCUA released its routine Q1 2026 system performance data for federally insured credit unions. This is an administrative data publication with no regulatory change or new requirement; useful for benchmarking but not actionable.
5a4edc2e04679438.title: NCUA Releases First Quarter 2026 Credit Union System Performance Data

7625a68bff840c3d.plain_english: NCUA issued a clarification on federal credit unions' powers over certain operations and activities. Credit unions are in scope, but the summary provides no fintech-relevant detail and limited specifics on what powers are being clarified.
7625a68bff840c3d.title: NCUA Clarifies Federal Credit Unions’ Power

79c121d1b88cf308.plain_english: NCUA finalized a rule on dependent care and board member reimbursement, an internal administrative matter for the NCUA Board. It has no bearing on community banks or fintechs.
79c121d1b88cf308.title: NCUA Board Approves Final Rule on Dependent Care and Board Member Reimbursement

3de88469a13d90dd.plain_english: Federal prudential regulators are asking financial institutions to detect and report suspicious activity tied to illegal immigration, effectively adding a BSA/AML monitoring and SAR-filing focus area. This affects compliance and transaction-monitoring programs broadly, including community banks and fintechs with money-movement functions.
3de88469a13d90dd.title: Federal Prudential Regulators Ask Financial Institutions to Detect and Report Illicit Activity Related to Illegal Aliens

e55d9104909e32fd.plain_english: NCUA announced a routine merger of Beverly Hills City Employees FCU into Nuvision FCU. It's a standard credit-union consolidation with no rule change or broader impact.
e55d9104909e32fd.title: Beverly Hills City Employees Federal Credit Union Merges into Nuvision Federal Credit Union

cff18924c942b057.plain_english: The NCUA barred five named individuals from working at any federally insured depository institution. It's a routine individual enforcement action with no policy or fintech implications.
cff18924c942b057.title: NCUA Prohibits Five Individuals from Participating in the Affairs of Any Federally Insured Depository Institution

c4080012794b8724.plain_english: The FFIEC and joint agencies are seeking comment on changes to the financial institutions rating system (likely CAMELS-related supervisory ratings). This is general supervisory framework guidance that would apply to community banks; there is no fintech-specific angle in the notice.
c4080012794b8724.title: Agencies Request Comment on Financial Institutions Rating System

f976c43b7b9df575.plain_english: NCUA proposed a rule setting standards for permitted payment stablecoin issuers, addressing how credit unions may interact with or issue stablecoins. This is a stablecoin/digital-asset item relevant to fintechs and any bank-fintech arrangement touching stablecoin issuance or payments, though it originates in the credit-union framework.
f976c43b7b9df575.title: NCUA Announces Proposed Rule for Permitted Payment Stablecoin Issuer Standards

08f57296f9f94d2d.plain_english: NCUA Chairman participated in a financial literacy roundtable. This is a routine public-engagement event with no regulatory or compliance impact.
08f57296f9f94d2d.title: NCUA Chairman Joins Leaders at Financial Literacy Roundtable

80a4e1c3db5985ef.plain_english: NCUA placed Jackson Area Federal Credit Union into conservatorship, taking over management to address safety-and-soundness or operational issues. This is a single-institution administrative action affecting that credit union's members; no rule change or broader industry obligation results.
80a4e1c3db5985ef.title: Jackson Area Federal Credit Union Conserved

f4bc08f05105a6e1.plain_english: NCUA announced its eleventh round of deregulation proposals for credit unions, but the summary provides no specifics on what rules would change. Credit unions should watch for details as proposals are released.
f4bc08f05105a6e1.title: NCUA Announces Eleventh Round of Deregulation Proposals

151244266802f2f3.plain_english: NCUA announced the closure of People Trust Community Federal Credit Union, a single-institution administrative action. Credit unions are in scope, but this has no broader rulemaking or systemic implications and affects only members of the closed institution.
151244266802f2f3.title: People Trust Community Federal Credit Union Closes

0ef73aa71fa6c30b.plain_english: NCUA is requesting comment on ways to enhance and streamline its data collection from federally-insured credit unions. This is an administrative process matter that could affect reporting burdens for credit unions in scope, but no specific rule change is detailed.
0ef73aa71fa6c30b.title: NCUA Seeks Comment on Enhancing and Streamlining Data Collection

cb84d0e948bdba49.plain_english: NCUA announced its tenth round of deregulation proposals affecting credit union operations and activities. Credit unions are in scope, but the notice is high-level with no specifics on which rules change or fintech/community-bank impact yet stated. Watch for the actual proposed rule text and comment periods.
cb84d0e948bdba49.title: NCUA Announces Tenth Round of Deregulation Proposals

e2dc295b2a4a105b.plain_english: The NCUA Board received briefings on brokered/reciprocal deposit FAQs, a deregulation project, and its strategic and annual performance plans. Nothing was adopted; this is a board-meeting update signaling potential future guidance on brokered deposits and deregulatory direction for credit unions.
e2dc295b2a4a105b.title: NCUA Board Briefed on Brokered and Reciprocal Deposits FAQs, Deregulation Project, Strategic Plan, and Annual Performance Plan

fbf11f8f04e362bc.plain_english: NCUA rolled out a ninth batch of deregulation proposals aimed at easing credit union operational and activity requirements. Credit unions in scope should watch for specifics as proposals are published, though no fintech or community-bank implications are stated.
fbf11f8f04e362bc.title: NCUA Announces Ninth Round of Deregulation Proposals

0354822a8e5f4367.plain_english: NCUA announced a routine board meeting scheduled for April 9. This is an administrative notice with no substantive rule or guidance content.
0354822a8e5f4367.title: NCUA Board to Hold Meeting on April 9

9dad2b4e77918507.plain_english: NCUA published its 2025 annual report, a routine administrative recap of the agency's operations. It carries no new requirements and is credit-union-focused administrative content.
9dad2b4e77918507.title: NCUA Releases 2025 Annual Report

68aa2ccee7688876.plain_english: Copper & Glass credit union closed and OMEGA Federal Credit Union assumed its loans, assets, and shares. This is a routine NCUA-facilitated purchase-and-assumption affecting only members of the closed institution; no rules changed.
68aa2ccee7688876.title: Copper & Glass Closes; OMEGA Federal Credit Union Assumes Loans, Assets, Shares

9713aa679b14db9a.plain_english: NCUA issued prohibition orders barring two individuals from participating in the affairs of any federally insured depository institution. This is a routine credit-union enforcement action with no bearing on community bank or fintech operations.
9713aa679b14db9a.title: NCUA Prohibits Two Individuals from Participating in the Affairs of Any Federally Insured Depository Institution

50ec14f65e4da8e1.plain_english: NCUA launched a new online system for processing credit union charter applications and field-of-membership requests. This is an administrative modernization affecting credit unions seeking to charter or expand membership; it changes the submission mechanism, not the underlying rules.
50ec14f65e4da8e1.title: NCUA Releases New Charter System

56760f875ad483a5.plain_english: NCUA announced an eighth round of deregulation proposals for credit unions. Details are sparse and no specifics were tied to BaaS, payments, or fintech partnerships, but credit unions are in scope on the same footing as community banks. Worth tracking for the substance once the actual proposals are published.
56760f875ad483a5.title: NCUA Announces Eighth Round of Deregulation Proposals

3da0d63855125d20.plain_english: NCUA released its routine Q4 2025 state-level credit union industry data report. It's a statistical release with no regulatory or compliance implications, useful only as background system-performance data.
3da0d63855125d20.title: NCUA Releases Q4 2025 State-level Credit Union Data Report

658d2082af4678fc.plain_english: NCUA rolled out its seventh round of deregulation proposals targeting credit union operations and activities. Credit unions should watch for specifics as proposals are detailed, but no fintech or community-bank-specific impact is stated in this text.
658d2082af4678fc.title: NCUA Announces Seventh Round of Deregulation Proposals

1f598d09fe5a6508.plain_english: NCUA published its routine Q4 2025 credit union system performance data. This is industry statistics only, with no rule change or supervisory action. Useful for benchmarking credit union financial trends but requires no action.
1f598d09fe5a6508.title: NCUA Releases Fourth Quarter 2025 Credit Union System Performance Data
