4c8abfe4885b1685.plain_english: NCUA launched a voluntary EGRPRA-style regulatory review as part of a broader deregulation push aimed at cutting compliance burden on credit unions. This is an early-stage administrative review with no immediate rule changes, but credit unions may get opportunities to flag burdensome regulations for revision.
4c8abfe4885b1685.title: NCUA Issues Voluntary EGRPRA Review; Continues Deregulation Project

7c0738f67013f667.plain_english: NCUA published its routine Q4 2025 Share Insurance Fund financial results. This is a system performance data release with no compliance or regulatory changes for credit unions.
7c0738f67013f667.title: NCUA Issues Share Insurance Fund Results for Fourth Quarter 2025

96562546323ebec3.plain_english: NCUA and FDIC are co-hosting an educational webinar on financial scams. This is a routine outreach event with no regulatory obligations or changes.
96562546323ebec3.title: NCUA and FDIC to Host Webinar on Financial Scams

9563a85546a473aa.plain_english: NCUA announced a sixth round of deregulation proposals affecting credit union operations and activities. Credit unions should watch for the specific proposals, but no fintech or bank-specific impact is stated and details are not yet provided.
9563a85546a473aa.title: NCUA Announces Sixth Round of Deregulation Proposals

762a46e8f18563e1.plain_english: FinCEN granted exceptive relief from a BSA requirement specifically for credit unions. This affects credit unions' AML compliance obligations, though the summary does not specify which requirement is being relaxed.
762a46e8f18563e1.title: FinCEN Issues Exceptive Relief on a Bank Secrecy Act Requirement for Credit Unions

12b44f5907f63afe.plain_english: NCUA announced its funds received clean audit opinions, a routine administrative/operational disclosure. No regulatory change or obligation for banks or fintechs.
12b44f5907f63afe.title: NCUA’s Funds Receive Clean Audit Opinions

522112a13c9000bf.plain_english: NCUA expanded its grant programs for newly chartered credit unions and urgent needs. This is an administrative grant funding program with no regulatory or supervisory change.
522112a13c9000bf.title: NCUA Expands Newly Chartered and Urgent Needs Grants

5c5a279988377349.plain_english: NCUA proposed a rule establishing an application process for credit unions or affiliated entities seeking to become permitted payment stablecoin issuers. This ties into the GENIUS Act framework for stablecoin issuance and matters to fintechs pursuing stablecoin activity through credit union channels.
5c5a279988377349.title: NCUA Proposes Rule for Permitted Payment Stablecoin Issuer Applications

8a10c577e0d52af2.plain_english: NCUA announced a fifth round of deregulation proposals aimed at credit union operations and activities. Details are not provided in the summary, but this is a credit-union-specific rulemaking effort worth tracking for regulatory relief affecting federally-insured credit unions.
8a10c577e0d52af2.title: NCUA Announces Fifth Round of Deregulation Proposals

6494215f5234baa5.plain_english: The NCUA Board voted to keep the maximum permissible interest rate ceiling for federal credit union loans at its elevated level rather than let it revert to the statutory 15%. This is a routine, recurring administrative action affecting federal credit unions' lending flexibility.
6494215f5234baa5.title: NCUA Board Extends Loan Interest Rate Ceiling

fee3801da8195748.plain_english: NCUA announced it will stay open and that share insurance remains in effect during a partial government shutdown. This is an administrative reassurance with no regulatory change.
fee3801da8195748.title: NCUA to Remain Open, Credit Union Members’ Shares Insured, During Partial Federal Government Shutdown

0c804f1fa6fe2e44.plain_english: NCUA Chairman Hauptman issued a statement on an appointment to the PCAOB. This is a personnel/administrative announcement with no regulatory substance affecting community banks or fintechs.
0c804f1fa6fe2e44.title: Chairman Hauptman’s Statement on Appointment to PCAOB

267b9dfd0b00bd16.plain_english: NCUA announced its 2026 leadership team, a routine personnel/administrative announcement. No regulatory changes or requirements are involved.
267b9dfd0b00bd16.title: NCUA Announces 2026 Leadership Team

53c6e5f1ee19efb2.plain_english: NCUA announced its fourth round of deregulation proposals affecting credit union operations and activities. Credit unions are in scope on the same footing as community banks, so this matters, though the summary provides no specifics on which rules are being loosened.
53c6e5f1ee19efb2.title: NCUA Announces Fourth Round of Deregulation Proposals

f6661cfb19e8ddbb.plain_english: NCUA placed Beverly Hills City Employees FCU into conservatorship, taking over management of a single small credit union. Since credit unions are in scope on the same footing as community banks, this is a relevant CU-specific administrative action, though it has no broader rule or systemic implications.
f6661cfb19e8ddbb.title: Beverly Hills City Employees Federal Credit Union Conserved

d932d3b59c198900.plain_english: The NCUA Board received briefings on an internal dependent care reimbursement rule and the Central Liquidity Facility budget. These are internal administrative and agency-budget matters with no operational impact on credit unions, community banks, or fintechs.
d932d3b59c198900.title: NCUA Board Briefed on Proposed Dependent Care Reimbursement Rule and Central Liquidity Facility Budget

00c2233f86cea907.plain_english: The NCUA placed People Trust Community Federal Credit Union into conservatorship, taking control of the institution's operations. This is a credit-union-specific administrative action; members and the CU are directly affected, but there is no broader rule change.
00c2233f86cea907.title: People Trust Community Federal Credit Union Conserved

2511b301312e8f1d.plain_english: NCUA announced a board meeting scheduled for January 22 with no substantive agenda details provided. This is a routine administrative notice with no policy content.
2511b301312e8f1d.title: NCUA Board to Hold Meeting on January 22

52ca27c26442ae7d.plain_english: NCUA released its annual supervisory priorities letter identifying 2026 exam focus areas for credit unions. Credit union leadership and compliance teams should align risk management and audit preparation with the highlighted areas.
52ca27c26442ae7d.title: NCUA Issues 2026 Supervisory Priorities Letter to Credit Unions

cca39f6b17dfb213.plain_english: NCUA announced a third round of deregulation proposals affecting credit union operations and activities. Credit unions are in scope, but the summary provides no specifics on which rules are targeted, so the practical impact is unclear pending the actual proposal text.
cca39f6b17dfb213.title: NCUA Announces Third Round of Deregulation Proposals

5e1787f4ca1fba65.title: NCUA Prohibits Three Individuals from Participating in the Affairs of Any Federally Insured Depository Institution

5e4631e4d0100b39.plain_english: NCUA announced a second round of deregulation proposals affecting credit union operations and activities. Federally-insured credit unions are in scope for us; watch for the specific rule text to assess impact. No fintech-specific details are provided.
5e4631e4d0100b39.title: NCUA Announces Second Round of Deregulation Proposals

33d0750f47d431f3.plain_english: The FDIC proposes CIP (customer identification program) requirements for permitted payment stablecoin issuers, likely implementing a stablecoin regulatory framework. This directly affects entities issuing payment stablecoins and the banks or fintechs sponsoring or partnering with them, imposing KYC/CIP-style onboarding obligations.
33d0750f47d431f3.title: Notice of Proposed Rulemaking: Permitted Payment Stablecoin Issuer Customer Identification Program
33d0750f47d431f3.fr_title: Permitted Payment Stablecoin Issuer Customer Identification Program
33d0750f47d431f3.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received by August 21, 2026.

8c832720f5a6b5f2.plain_english: The FDIC's Office of Ombudsman released its 2025 annual report, a routine administrative publication summarizing the office's activities. It carries no new requirements or supervisory expectations.
8c832720f5a6b5f2.title: FDIC’s Office of Ombudsman Publishes 2025 Annual Report

671c225de8f63d37.plain_english: Routine annual FDIC notice reminding insured institutions to file the Summary of Deposits survey reporting branch-level deposit data as of June 30, 2026. This is a standard administrative reporting obligation for all insured banks.
671c225de8f63d37.title: Summary of Deposits Survey and Filing for June 30, 2026

70572bd3a3a9f3ae.plain_english: The FDIC proposes revisions to the Uniform Financial Institutions Rating System (CAMELS) and related examination procedures. This is general supervisory rating methodology affecting how examiners assess all insured institutions, not a fintech-specific measure.
70572bd3a3a9f3ae.title: Proposed Revisions to the Uniform Financial Institutions Rating System
70572bd3a3a9f3ae.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received by August 17, 2026.

1b2330f39c85391e.plain_english: The FDIC updated its Q&A on official signs and advertising rules, which govern how insured status must be displayed both physically and digitally. This matters for bank-fintech partnerships and neobank apps, where the FDIC has been focused on misrepresentation of deposit insurance and proper disclosure on digital channels and non-bank interfaces.
1b2330f39c85391e.title: Updated Questions and Answers Regarding FDIC Official Signs and Advertising Requirements

46f08dfb7e2ff3ef.plain_english: The FDIC issued routine disaster-related supervisory relief for institutions in areas of the Northern Mariana Islands hit by Typhoon Sinlaku. This is standard regulatory accommodation for affected banks and does not relate to our focus areas.
46f08dfb7e2ff3ef.title: Supervisory Relief to Help Financial Institutions and Facilitate Recovery in Areas of the Northern Mariana Islands Affected by Typhoon Sinlaku

4729ca28fe41b1ef.plain_english: FDIC issued routine disaster-related supervisory relief for institutions in weather-affected areas of Hawaii, encouraging accommodations for affected borrowers. This is standard regional regulatory relief with no fintech or BaaS implications.
4729ca28fe41b1ef.title: Supervisory Relief to Help Financial Institutions and Facilitate Recovery in Areas of Hawaii Affected by Low Weather Systems

3c2405b538302dc4.plain_english: The FDIC finalized revisions to the Community Bank Leverage Ratio framework, the optional simplified capital regime for qualifying banks under $10B. This is a capital/safety-and-soundness change affecting community banks that opt into CBLR, not a fintech-specific matter.
3c2405b538302dc4.title: Final Rule on Revisions to the Community Bank Leverage Ratio (CBLR) Framework
3c2405b538302dc4.fr_title: Regulatory Capital Rule: Revisions to the Community Bank Leverage Ratio Framework
3c2405b538302dc4.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received by January 30, 2026.

65b183ca7682295d.plain_english: The FDIC issued routine disaster-related supervisory relief for institutions in Tennessee areas affected by a severe winter storm. This is standard localized regulatory accommodation, not a substantive rule change.
65b183ca7682295d.title: Supervisory Relief to Help Financial Institutions and Facilitate Recovery in Areas of Tennessee Affected by Severe Winter Storm

1373766c84235dd7.plain_english: The FDIC issued routine disaster-relief guidance for institutions serving areas of Mississippi hit by a severe winter storm, encouraging accommodations for affected borrowers. This is standard regional relief, not a substantive rule change.
1373766c84235dd7.title: Supervisory Relief to Help Financial Institutions and Facilitate Recovery in Areas of Mississippi Affected by Severe Winter Storm

84093b5b1dfeab7c.plain_english: The FDIC issued routine disaster-relief guidance encouraging supervisory flexibility for institutions serving areas of Washington hit by severe storms and flooding. This is a localized, administrative disaster-relief notice with no substantive regulatory change.
84093b5b1dfeab7c.title: Supervisory Relief to Help Financial Institutions and Facilitate Recovery in Areas of Washington Affected by Severe Storms, Straight-line Winds, Flooding, Landslides, and Mudslides

9de1e35eae562bde.plain_english: The agencies revised the interagency model risk management guidance, which governs how banks develop, validate and control models used for credit, risk, and compliance decisions. Community banks relying on models (including vendor-provided ones) should review validation and governance practices against the updated expectations.
9de1e35eae562bde.title: Agencies Revise the Interagency Model Risk Management Guidance

8c0463e56623e4c9.plain_english: Routine monthly FDIC publication of CRA examination ratings for state nonmember banks evaluated in March 2026. Purely administrative disclosure with no new requirements.
8c0463e56623e4c9.title: FDIC Issues List of Banks Examined for CRA Compliance

b2c98703d01a2169.plain_english: Routine monthly FDIC publication of CRA examination ratings for state nonmember banks evaluated in February 2026. No new requirements or policy changes.
b2c98703d01a2169.title: FDIC Issues List of Banks Examined for CRA Compliance

357225bd01ea6bb8.plain_english: Routine annual inflation adjustment to Regulation Z dollar thresholds for credit card penalty fees, HOEPA high-cost mortgage triggers, and qualified mortgage points-and-fees limits. Any lender extending consumer credit under TILA needs to update thresholds for the coming year, but this is a mechanical recalculation, not a substantive change.
357225bd01ea6bb8.title: Truth in Lending (Regulation Z) Annual Threshold Adjustments (Credit Cards, HOEPA, and Qualified Mortgages)
357225bd01ea6bb8.fr_title: Truth in Lending (Regulation Z) Annual Threshold Adjustments (Credit Cards, HOEPA, and Qualified Mortgages)
357225bd01ea6bb8.fr_dates_text: This final rule is effective January 1, 2026.

730f3f0cb8f5989a.plain_english: The CFPB finalized its June 2025 interim rule extending the compliance deadlines for the 1071 small business lending data collection rule under Regulation B, along with other adjustments. This gives small business lenders, including community banks, more time before they must collect and report demographic and application data on small business credit.
730f3f0cb8f5989a.title: Small Business Lending under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act (Regulation B); Extension of Compliance Dates
730f3f0cb8f5989a.fr_title: Small Business Lending Under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act (Regulation B); Extension of Compliance Dates
730f3f0cb8f5989a.fr_dates_text: This final rule is effective December 1, 2025.

1638731ab2fca40c.plain_english: FinCEN delayed by two years the effective date of AML/CFT program and SAR filing requirements for registered investment advisers and exempt reporting advisers. This affects investment advisers, not community banks or fintechs in our scope.
1638731ab2fca40c.effective_or_comment_date: Delayed two years (specific date not stated)
1638731ab2fca40c.title: Delaying the Effective Date of the Anti-Money Laundering/Countering the Financing of Terrorism Program and Suspicious Activity Report Filing Requirements for Registered Investment Advisers and Exempt Reporting Advisers

620e11814b3a80f4.plain_english: FinCEN is renewing without change its existing 314(a)-style information-sharing recordkeeping requirements under the BSA and seeking PRA comments on burden. No substantive obligations change; institutions must still search records and report matches when FinCEN requests. Routine administrative renewal with no action needed beyond optional comment.
620e11814b3a80f4.title: Agency Information Collection Activities; Proposed Renewal; Comment Request; Renewal Without Change on Information Sharing Between Government Agencies and Financial Institutions
620e11814b3a80f4.fr_title: Agency Information Collection Activities; Proposed Renewal; Comment Request; Renewal Without Change on Information Sharing Between Government Agencies and Financial Institutions
620e11814b3a80f4.fr_dates_text: Written comments are welcome and must be received on or before December 1, 2025.

e7a0f10a4a4f35f9.plain_english: FinCEN issued a new Geographic Targeting Order requiring certain MSBs along the southwest border to report, retain records of, and verify identity for cash transactions of $1,000 to $10,000. This raises the reporting threshold from the prior GTO's $200 floor, but MSBs in the covered areas face heightened recordkeeping and CIP obligations on small-dollar cash flows.
e7a0f10a4a4f35f9.title: Geographic Targeting Order Imposing Recordkeeping and Reporting Requirements on Certain Money Services Businesses Along the Southwest Border
e7a0f10a4a4f35f9.fr_title: Geographic Targeting Order Imposing Recordkeeping and Reporting Requirements on Certain Money Services Businesses Along the Southwest Border
e7a0f10a4a4f35f9.fr_dates_text: Effective date: This action takes effect March 7, 2026.

613463b3871f06ae.plain_english: FinCEN is seeking comment on a routine Paperwork Reduction Act renewal of existing BSA reporting requirements for transactions with foreign financial agencies, with no substantive changes to the collection itself (only burden-estimation methodology tweaks). This is an administrative notice with no new compliance obligations.
613463b3871f06ae.title: Agency Information Collection Activities; Proposed Renewal; Comment Request; Renewal Without Change of Reports of Transactions With Foreign Financial Agencies

23e68725167a551b.plain_english: Routine Paperwork Reduction Act notice renewing existing BSA recordkeeping requirements on correspondent accounts for foreign banks and the shell-bank prohibition, without any change. It affects institutions holding foreign correspondent accounts but imposes no new obligations.
23e68725167a551b.title: Agency Information Collection Activities; Proposed Renewal; Comment Request; Renewal Without Change of Prohibition on Correspondent Accounts for Foreign Shell Banks; Records Concerning Owners of Foreign Banks and Agents for Service of Legal Process

af54fe68af41546b.plain_english: FinCEN is seeking comment on the routine PRA renewal of BSA recordkeeping requirements for casinos and card clubs, with no substantive changes. This is an administrative paperwork notice affecting casinos, not banks or fintechs.
af54fe68af41546b.title: Agency Information Collection Activities; Proposed Renewal; Comment Request; Renewal Without Change of Additional Records To Be Made and Retained by Casinos

297f33fe8fa6a455.plain_english: FinCEN is seeking comment on renewing, without change, an existing BSA information collection requiring US banks with correspondent accounts for certain foreign banks to query and report on transactions involving Iranian- and IRGC-linked designated persons. This is a routine Paperwork Reduction Act renewal with no substantive change, affecting only institutions maintaining relevant correspondent accounts.
297f33fe8fa6a455.title: Agency Information Collection Activities; Proposed Renewal; Comment Request; Renewal Without Change of Reporting Obligations on Foreign Bank Relationships With Iranian-Linked Financial Institutions Designated Under IEEPA and IRGC-Linked Persons Designated Under IEEPA

e24b987127b9c569.plain_english: FinCEN made its annual inflation adjustment to maximum civil money penalties for BSA/AML violations. This is a routine statutory recalibration—no new obligations—but it raises the financial exposure for any BSA/AML enforcement action.
e24b987127b9c569.title: Financial Crimes Enforcement Network; Inflation Adjustment of Civil Monetary Penalties
e24b987127b9c569.fr_title: Financial Crimes Enforcement Network; Inflation Adjustment of Civil Monetary Penalties

afd8606883a5ecd1.plain_english: FinCEN is soliciting nominations for membership in its Bank Secrecy Act Advisory Group, with three-year terms. This is a routine administrative membership solicitation with no substantive regulatory change.
afd8606883a5ecd1.title: Bank Secrecy Act Advisory Group; Solicitation of Application for Membership

bf2ab9f71ddee5ae.plain_english: FinCEN finalized a rule adding certain registered investment advisers and exempt reporting advisers to the BSA definition of 'financial institution,' requiring them to maintain AML/CFT programs and file SARs. This targets the investment adviser sector, not community banks, BaaS arrangements, or the fintech verticals we track.
bf2ab9f71ddee5ae.title: Financial Crimes Enforcement Network: Anti-Money Laundering/Countering the Financing of Terrorism Program and Suspicious Activity Report Filing Requirements for Registered Investment Advisers and Exempt Reporting Advisers

fb9f8cc9e6bb5acd.plain_english: FinCEN finalized reporting and recordkeeping requirements for professionals involved in non-financed residential real estate transfers to legal entities and trusts, aimed at curbing money laundering in real estate. It targets title/settlement and closing professionals, not banks or fintechs, and non-financed transfers fall outside typical lending or BaaS activities.
fb9f8cc9e6bb5acd.title: Anti-Money Laundering Regulations for Residential Real Estate Transfers

3e25c7c388604ac8.plain_english: FinCEN proposes to modernize AML/CFT program requirements under the AML Act of 2020, mandating effective, risk-based programs with a formal risk assessment process and requiring institutions to incorporate government-wide AML priorities. It applies broadly to all covered financial institutions, including community banks and MSBs, and would harmonize program rules across institution types. This is general BSA/AML rulemaking rather than a fintech-specific measure, though MSBs and BaaS partners will need to align programs.
3e25c7c388604ac8.title: Anti-Money Laundering and Countering the Financing of Terrorism Programs
3e25c7c388604ac8.fr_title: Anti-Money Laundering and Countering the Financing of Terrorism Programs
3e25c7c388604ac8.fr_dates_text: Comments must be submitted on or before September 8, 2026.

ef71257434c5a784.plain_english: FinCEN made its annual inflation adjustment to the maximum civil monetary penalties it can impose, including for BSA/AML violations. This is a routine statutory update but raises the ceiling on potential fines for compliance failures.
ef71257434c5a784.title: Financial Crimes Enforcement Network; Inflation Adjustment of Civil Monetary Penalties
ef71257434c5a784.fr_title: Financial Crimes Enforcement Network; Inflation Adjustment of Civil Monetary Penalties

084afef9ae74f4c9.plain_english: FinCEN is soliciting nominations for membership in its Bank Secrecy Act Advisory Group for three-year terms. This is a routine administrative membership notice with no new compliance obligations.
084afef9ae74f4c9.title: Bank Secrecy Act Advisory Group; Solicitation of Application for Membership

dae4ddd65eb83be4.plain_english: FinCEN extended the initial BOI filing deadline from 30 to 90 days for reporting companies created or registered during 2024, giving new entities more time to comply. This is a CTA reporting mechanics change affecting newly formed entities, not a bank obligation per se, though it touches the broader beneficial-ownership regime BSA-covered institutions operate within.
dae4ddd65eb83be4.effective_or_comment_date: Applies to entities created/registered on or after January 1, 2024 and before January 1, 2025
dae4ddd65eb83be4.title: Beneficial Ownership Information Reporting Deadline Extension for Reporting Companies Created or Registered in 2024
dae4ddd65eb83be4.fr_title: Beneficial Ownership Information Reporting Deadline Extension for Reporting Companies Created or Registered in 2024

3ff91be9464058d2.plain_english: FinCEN is establishing a Privacy Act system of records to house beneficial ownership information collected under the Corporate Transparency Act. This is an administrative privacy notice supporting BOI reporting infrastructure rather than a substantive new obligation; entities subject to CTA reporting are affected only indirectly.
3ff91be9464058d2.title: Privacy Act of 1974; System of Records
3ff91be9464058d2.fr_title: Privacy Act of 1974; System of Records
3ff91be9464058d2.fr_dates_text: EPA stopped maintaining this system of records in July 2024. Persons wishing to comment on this system or records notice must do so by April 22, 2026.

b93884b90074993c.plain_english: FinCEN is proposing to renew, without changes, its MSB registration requirement and Form 107, and is seeking comment under the Paperwork Reduction Act. No substantive obligations change for money services businesses, who must still register, renew every two years, and maintain agent lists.
b93884b90074993c.title: Agency Information Collection Activities; Proposed Renewal; Comment Request; Renewal Without Change of the Registration of Money Services Businesses Regulation and FinCEN Form 107
b93884b90074993c.fr_title: Agency Information Collection Activities; Proposed Renewal; Comment Request; Renewal Without Change of the Registration of Money Services Businesses Regulation and FinCEN Form 107
b93884b90074993c.fr_dates_text: Written comments are welcome and must be received on or before June 29, 2026.

cf441611bc184f8b.plain_english: FinCEN is soliciting nominations for membership on its Bank Secrecy Act Advisory Group, an administrative call for applicants to fill three-year terms. This is a routine membership solicitation with no new compliance obligations.
cf441611bc184f8b.title: Bank Secrecy Act Advisory Group; Solicitation of Application for Membership

6980d4948ce6fc04.plain_english: FinCEN made its annual inflation adjustment to civil monetary penalty amounts under the Bank Secrecy Act. This raises the maximum penalties for BSA/AML violations but imposes no new substantive obligations; it affects all BSA-regulated entities.
6980d4948ce6fc04.title: Financial Crimes Enforcement Network; Inflation Adjustment of Civil Monetary Penalties
6980d4948ce6fc04.fr_title: Financial Crimes Enforcement Network; Inflation Adjustment of Civil Monetary Penalties

52c276b14fecf57a.plain_english: FinCEN made its annual inflation adjustment to the maximum civil monetary penalties it can impose for BSA/AML violations. This is a routine statutory update that raises the ceiling on potential fines but changes no compliance obligations.
52c276b14fecf57a.title: Financial Crimes Enforcement Network; Inflation Adjustment of Civil Monetary Penalties
52c276b14fecf57a.fr_title: Financial Crimes Enforcement Network; Inflation Adjustment of Civil Monetary Penalties

eafcb75695fd1ee7.plain_english: FinCEN finalized the Corporate Transparency Act beneficial ownership reporting rule, requiring most legal entities to report their beneficial owners and company applicants directly to FinCEN. This is a BSA/AML-adjacent obligation on reporting companies themselves rather than on banks' CDD processes, though it feeds the broader AML framework banks and fintechs operate under.
eafcb75695fd1ee7.title: Beneficial Ownership Information Reporting Requirements
eafcb75695fd1ee7.fr_title: Beneficial Ownership Information Reporting Requirement Revision and Deadline Extension
eafcb75695fd1ee7.fr_dates_text: This rule is effective March 26, 2025. Written comments must be received on or before May 27, 2025.

c645d412c0fe7e38.plain_english: FinCEN extended the comment period on an ANPRM exploring potential BSA reporting requirements for persons involved in real estate transactions. This targets the real estate sector, not community bank or fintech operations.
c645d412c0fe7e38.title: Anti-Money Laundering Regulations for Real Estate Transactions

282a505a3fdce000.plain_english: FinCEN is soliciting nominations for membership on the Bank Secrecy Act Advisory Group for three-year terms. This is a routine administrative membership solicitation with no new compliance obligations.
282a505a3fdce000.title: Bank Secrecy Act Advisory Group; Solicitation of Application for Membership

8c06369a495d362c.plain_english: Nine financial agencies finalized joint data standards under the Financial Data Transparency Act to make regulatory data interoperable across agencies. The rule sets standards but does not itself change any reporting requirements—those come later through separate rulemakings. No immediate action is needed at the effective date.
8c06369a495d362c.title: Financial Data Transparency Act Joint Data Standards
8c06369a495d362c.fr_title: Financial Data Transparency Act Joint Data Standards
8c06369a495d362c.fr_dates_text: Effective date: The joint rule is effective on October 1, 2026. At the effective date, the joint rule will not change any reporting requirements without further action by the agencies.

20927210b9d41f71.plain_english: NCUA proposes to remove duplicative cross-reference provisions from its share insurance regulations, a purely administrative cleanup that changes no compliance obligations. Affects federally-insured credit unions only, with no substantive impact.
20927210b9d41f71.title: Requirements for Insurance
20927210b9d41f71.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received by March 30, 2026.

1c60f5545462cd4d.plain_english: Routine NCUA Paperwork Reduction Act notice submitting information collection requests to OMB for review. This is a credit-union administrative matter with no substantive regulatory change.
1c60f5545462cd4d.title: Renewal of Agency Information Collections for Comments Request: Proposed Collections

d0a3c663398aca05.plain_english: NCUA published a routine Sunshine Act meeting notice, an administrative announcement of a scheduled board meeting. It carries no regulatory substance or obligations.
d0a3c663398aca05.title: Sunshine Act Meetings

96336fafdb1a6dcb.plain_english: Routine NCUA administrative notice of a Sunshine Act meeting. No regulatory change or substantive content affecting the areas we monitor.
96336fafdb1a6dcb.title: Sunshine Act Meetings

560bbf5126980cea.plain_english: NCUA proposes to streamline Part 749 governing credit union vital records preservation and catastrophic-act preparedness, updating definitions and removing outdated appendices. Applies only to federally-insured credit unions; it's an administrative modernization with no substantive new compliance burden.
560bbf5126980cea.title: Records Preservation Program and Appendices-Record Retention Guidelines; Catastrophic Act Preparedness Guidelines
560bbf5126980cea.fr_title: Records Preservation Program and Appendices-Record Retention Guidelines; Catastrophic Act Preparedness Guidelines
560bbf5126980cea.fr_dates_text: This final rule is effective on July 16, 2026.

5b931ddefd7b43c5.plain_english: The NCUA is asking for comment on which of its credit-union regulations—including BSA/AML rules—are outdated or unduly burdensome, as part of a routine EGRPRA review. This is an early-stage input request affecting federally-insured credit unions; no rules change yet, but it's an opportunity to flag burdensome requirements.
5b931ddefd7b43c5.title: Regulatory Publication and Voluntary Review as Contemplated by the Economic Growth and Regulatory Paperwork Reduction Act of 1996
5b931ddefd7b43c5.fr_title: Regulatory Publication and Voluntary Review as Contemplated by the Economic Growth and Regulatory Paperwork Reduction Act of 1996
5b931ddefd7b43c5.fr_dates_text: Comments must be received by June 1, 2026.

a040b1dea025d549.plain_english: NCUA proposes to rescind IRPS 06-1 to consolidate the sources federal credit unions must consult for chartering and field-of-membership compliance. This is a credit-union-only chartering matter with no BaaS, payments or other fintech dimension.
